#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LOG_FILE="${SCRIPT_DIR}/cramfs_disable.log"

log_message() {
    local message="$1"
    echo "[$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $message" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

disable_cramfs_module() {
    log_message "Starting cramfs filesystem module disabling process..."
    
    MODPROBE_DIR="/etc/modprobe.d"
    MODPROBE_FILE="${MODPROBE_DIR}/cramfs-disable.conf"
    
    if [[ ! -d "$MODPROBE_DIR" ]]; then
        log_message "Creating $MODPROBE_DIR directory..."
        mkdir -p "$MODPROBE_DIR"
    fi
    
    if [[ ! -f "$MODPROBE_FILE" ]]; then
        log_message "Creating $MODPROBE_FILE to disable cramfs module..."
        cat > "$MODPROBE_FILE" << 'EOF'
# Disable cramfs filesystem support
install cramfs /bin/true
blacklist cramfs
EOF
        log_message "cramfs module configuration added to $MODPROBE_FILE"
    else
        log_message "$MODPROBE_FILE already exists, verifying contents..."
        if ! grep -q "install cramfs /bin/true" "$MODPROBE_FILE"; then
            log_message "Adding 'install cramfs /bin/true' to $MODPROBE_FILE..."
            echo "install cramfs /bin/true" >> "$MODPROBE_FILE"
        fi
        if ! grep -q "blacklist cramfs" "$MODPROBE_FILE"; then
            log_message "Adding 'blacklist cramfs' to $MODPROBE_FILE..."
            echo "blacklist cramfs" >> "$MODPROBE_FILE"
        fi
    fi
    
    INITRAMFS_DIR="/etc/initramfs-tools/conf.d"
    INITRAMFS_FILE="${INITRAMFS_DIR}/disable-cramfs.conf"
    
    if command -v dracut &> /dev/null; then
        log_message "System uses dracut for initramfs..."
        DRACUT_DIR="/etc/dracut.conf.d"
        DRACUT_FILE="${DRACUT_DIR}/disable-cramfs.conf"
        
        if [[ ! -d "$DRACUT_DIR" ]]; then
            log_message "Creating $DRACUT_DIR directory..."
            mkdir -p "$DRACUT_DIR"
        fi
        
        if [[ ! -f "$DRACUT_FILE" ]]; then
            log_message "Creating $DRACUT_FILE to exclude cramfs from initramfs..."
            echo "omit_drivers+=\" cramfs \"" > "$DRACUT_FILE"
            log_message "Regenerating initramfs with dracut..."
            if dracut -f; then
                log_message "Successfully regenerated initramfs"
            else
                log_message "WARNING: Failed to regenerate initramfs with dracut"
            fi
        else
            log_message "$DRACUT_FILE already exists"
        fi
    fi
    
    if [[ -d "$INITRAMFS_DIR" ]]; then
        log_message "System has initramfs-tools directory..."
        if [[ ! -f "$INITRAMFS_FILE" ]]; then
            log_message "Creating $INITRAMFS_FILE to exclude cramfs from initramfs..."
            echo "export CRAMFS=n" > "$INITRAMFS_FILE"
        fi
    fi
    
    if lsmod | grep -q "^cramfs "; then
        log_message "cramfs module is currently loaded. Attempting to unload..."
        if rmmod cramfs 2>/dev/null; then
            log_message "Successfully unloaded cramfs module"
        else
            log_message "WARNING: Could not unload cramfs module (may be in use)"
        fi
    else
        log_message "cramfs module is not currently loaded"
    fi
    
    log_message "Cramfs filesystem module has been disabled"
    log_message "Changes will persist across reboots"
    
    log_message "Verification of cramfs module configuration:"
    if [[ -f "$MODPROBE_FILE" ]]; then
        log_message "Contents of $MODPROBE_FILE:"
        cat "$MODPROBE_FILE" | while read -r line; do
            log_message "  $line"
        done
    fi
}

disable_cramfs_module

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure AIDE is installed on RHEL 8
# This script is idempotent and will only install AIDE if not already present

set -e

# Check if AIDE is already installed
if rpm -q aide > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "AIDE is already installed."
    exit 0
fi

# Install AIDE
echo "Installing AIDE..."
yum install -y aide

# Verify installation was successful
if rpm -q aide > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "AIDE has been successfully installed."
    exit 0
else
    echo "Failed to install AIDE."
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash

# RHEL 8 Audit Rule Configuration for fchmod System Calls
# This script configures the audit daemon to record fchmod system call usage
# for non-system user accounts (UID >= 1000) to detect unauthorized permission modifications

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULE_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit_fchmod.rules"
AUDIT_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"

# Ensure audit rules directory exists
if [ ! -d "${AUDIT_CONFIG_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${AUDIT_CONFIG_DIR}"
fi

# Function to check if audit daemon is running
check_audit_daemon() {
    if ! systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
        echo "Warning: audit daemon is not running. Starting it now..."
        systemctl start auditd || echo "Note: Audit daemon may require manual start"
    fi
}

# Function to load audit rules
load_audit_rules() {
    if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
        augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || true
    elif command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
        auditctl -R "${AUDIT_RULE_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
}

# Function to verify audit rules are loaded
verify_rules() {
    if command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
        auditctl -l | grep -q "fchmod" || echo "Warning: fchmod rules may not be loaded"
    fi
}

# Create the audit rule file
cat > "${AUDIT_RULE_FILE}" << 'EOF'
# Audit rules for fchmod system calls
# This rule captures all uses of fchmod (and fchmodat) syscalls
# for non-system user accounts (login UID >= 1000)
# These syscalls change discretionary access control permissions of open files

# fchmod syscall - 32-bit (i386)
-a exit,always -F arch=b32 -S fchmod -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k perm_mod_fchmod

# fchmod syscall - 64-bit (x86_64)
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S fchmod -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k perm_mod_fchmod

# fchmodat syscall - 32-bit (i386) - alternative to fchmod with AT_FDCWD
-a exit,always -F arch=b32 -S fchmodat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k perm_mod_fchmod

# fchmodat syscall - 64-bit (x86_64) - alternative to fchmod with AT_FDCWD
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S fchmodat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k perm_mod_fchmod

# Make configuration immutable to prevent tampering (optional)
-e 2
EOF

# Verify file was created successfully
if [ ! -f "${AUDIT_RULE_FILE}" ]; then
    echo "Error: Failed to create audit rule file"
    exit 1
fi

# Set appropriate permissions on the rule file
chmod 640 "${AUDIT_RULE_FILE}"
chown root:root "${AUDIT_RULE_FILE}"

# Load the audit rules
load_audit_rules

# Wait a moment for rules to load
sleep 1

# Verify the rules are loaded
verify_rules

# Ensure auditd service is enabled and started
systemctl enable auditd
check_audit_daemon

echo "Audit rules for fchmod have been successfully configured"
echo "Rules will persist across reboots"

# Display current fchmod rules
if command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
    echo ""
    echo "Currently loaded fchmod audit rules:"
    auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep "fchmod" || echo "Rules are pending auditd restart"
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Set strict error handling
set -euo pipefail

# Check if running on RHEL 8
if ! grep -q "^NAME=\"Red Hat Enterprise Linux\"" /etc/os-release 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Warning: This script is designed for RHEL 8 systems"
fi

# Define SSH config file
SSH_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSH_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"

# Create SSH config directory if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -d "$SSH_CONFIG_DIR" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$SSH_CONFIG_DIR"
    chmod 755 "$SSH_CONFIG_DIR"
fi

# Create a configuration file for entropy settings
ENTROPY_CONFIG="${SSH_CONFIG_DIR}/01-entropy-source.conf"

# Define the entropy configuration content
# Using RandSource to point to /dev/urandom for high-quality entropy
# and setting appropriate entropy parameters
ENTROPY_CONTENT="# High-entropy random number source configuration
# Ensures SSH uses high-quality entropy for cryptographic operations
RandSource /dev/urandom"

# Check if the configuration file exists and has the correct content
if [ -f "$ENTROPY_CONFIG" ]; then
    # Check if the current content matches what we want
    if grep -q "RandSource /dev/urandom" "$ENTROPY_CONFIG"; then
        echo "SSH entropy configuration already properly configured"
        exit 0
    fi
fi

# Write the entropy configuration
echo "Configuring SSH server entropy source..."
echo "$ENTROPY_CONTENT" > "$ENTROPY_CONFIG"
chmod 600 "$ENTROPY_CONFIG"

# Also ensure main sshd_config doesn't have conflicting settings
# Comment out any existing RandSource directives in main config
if grep -q "^RandSource" "$SSH_CONFIG" 2>/dev/null; then
    sed -i 's/^RandSource/#RandSource/g' "$SSH_CONFIG"
fi

# Verify SSH configuration syntax
if ! sshd -t &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Error: SSH configuration syntax error after changes"
    rm -f "$ENTROPY_CONFIG"
    exit 1
fi

echo "SSH entropy source successfully configured"

# Apply the configuration changes
systemctl reload sshd || systemctl restart sshd

# Verify that the entropy configuration is in place
if grep -q "RandSource /dev/urandom" "$ENTROPY_CONFIG"; then
    echo "Entropy configuration verified and applied successfully"
    exit 0
else
    echo "Error: Failed to apply entropy configuration"
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash

set -e

SSH_CONFIG_FILE="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"

# Check if the system is RHEL 8
if ! grep -q "^NAME=\"Red Hat Enterprise Linux\"" /etc/os-release 2>/dev/null; then
    if ! grep -q "RHEL" /etc/redhat-release 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Warning: This script is designed for RHEL 8 systems"
    fi
fi

# Create backup of the original sshd_config file
if [ ! -f "${SSH_CONFIG_FILE}.backup" ]; then
    cp "${SSH_CONFIG_FILE}" "${SSH_CONFIG_FILE}.backup"
fi

# Check if X11Forwarding is already disabled
if grep -q "^X11Forwarding no" "${SSH_CONFIG_FILE}"; then
    echo "X11Forwarding is already disabled in ${SSH_CONFIG_FILE}"
    exit 0
fi

# Remove any existing X11Forwarding settings (commented or not)
sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*#\?X11Forwarding/d' "${SSH_CONFIG_FILE}"

# Add the X11Forwarding no directive
echo "X11Forwarding no" >> "${SSH_CONFIG_FILE}"

echo "X11Forwarding has been disabled in ${SSH_CONFIG_FILE}"

# Validate the SSH configuration syntax
if ! sshd -t; then
    echo "Error: SSH configuration syntax is invalid. Restoring backup..."
    cp "${SSH_CONFIG_FILE}.backup" "${SSH_CONFIG_FILE}"
    exit 1
fi

echo "SSH configuration syntax is valid"

# Restart the SSH service to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

echo "SSH service has been restarted successfully"
echo "X11 graphical forwarding has been disabled and will persist across reboots"

#!/bin/bash

set -e

# Disable CAN protocol kernel module on RHEL 8
# This script ensures the CAN kernel module cannot be loaded

# Blacklist the CAN module to prevent loading
BLACKLIST_DIR="/etc/modprobe.d"
BLACKLIST_FILE="${BLACKLIST_DIR}/can-blacklist.conf"

# Create the directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p "$BLACKLIST_DIR"

# Check if the blacklist file already contains the CAN module entries
if ! grep -q "^blacklist can$" "$BLACKLIST_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    # Add blacklist entries for CAN and related modules
    echo "blacklist can" >> "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
    echo "blacklist can_raw" >> "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
    echo "blacklist can_bcm" >> "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
fi

# Check if the blacklist file already contains the install ban entries
if ! grep -q "^install can /bin/true$" "$BLACKLIST_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    # Add install ban entries to prevent loading even if blacklist is bypassed
    echo "install can /bin/true" >> "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
    echo "install can_raw /bin/true" >> "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
    echo "install can_bcm /bin/true" >> "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
fi

# Unload the CAN modules if they are currently loaded
for module in can_bcm can_raw can; do
    if lsmod | grep -q "^$module"; then
        modprobe -r "$module" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
done

echo "CAN protocol kernel module has been successfully disabled and blacklisted."
exit 0

#!/bin/bash

set -e

# Disable CAN protocol kernel module on RHEL 8
# This script ensures the CAN kernel module cannot be loaded

# Blacklist the CAN module to prevent loading
BLACKLIST_DIR="/etc/modprobe.d"
BLACKLIST_FILE="${BLACKLIST_DIR}/can-blacklist.conf"

# Create the directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p "$BLACKLIST_DIR"

# Check if the blacklist file already contains the CAN module entries
if ! grep -q "^blacklist can$" "$BLACKLIST_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    # Add blacklist entries for CAN and related modules
    echo "blacklist can" >> "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
    echo "blacklist can_raw" >> "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
    echo "blacklist can_bcm" >> "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
fi

# Check if the blacklist file already contains the install ban entries
if ! grep -q "^install can /bin/true$" "$BLACKLIST_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    # Add install ban entries to prevent loading even if blacklist is bypassed
    echo "install can /bin/true" >> "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
    echo "install can_raw /bin/true" >> "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
    echo "install can_bcm /bin/true" >> "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
fi

# Unload the CAN modules if they are currently loaded
for module in can_bcm can_raw can; do
    if lsmod | grep -q "^$module"; then
        modprobe -r "$module" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
done

# RHEL 8 Password Quality Policy Strengthening Script
# Enforces minimum of 4 character classes in passwords
# (uppercase, lowercase, digits, special characters)

# Function to check if a line exists in a file
line_in_file() {
    local line="$1"
    local file="$2"
    grep -Fxq "$line" "$file" 2>/dev/null || return 1
}

# Function to add or update a configuration parameter in a file
update_config_param() {
    local param="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local file="$3"
    
    if grep -q "^${param}" "$file"; then
        sed -i "s/^${param}=.*/${param}=${value}/" "$file"
    else
        echo "${param}=${value}" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Ensure required packages are installed
echo "Checking for required packages..."
if ! rpm -q libpwquality &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Installing libpwquality..."
    yum install -y libpwquality
fi

if ! rpm -q pam &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Installing pam..."
    yum install -y pam
fi

# Configure pwquality.conf for minimum 4 character classes
PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"

echo "Configuring ${PWQUALITY_CONF}..."

# Backup the original file if it hasn't been backed up yet
if [[ ! -f "${PWQUALITY_CONF}.orig" ]]; then
    cp "${PWQUALITY_CONF}" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}.orig"
fi

# Set minimum character classes to 4
update_config_param "minclass" "4" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"

# Set minimum password length (recommended to be at least 14 with 4 classes)
if grep -q "^minlen" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"; then
    current_minlen=$(grep "^minlen" "$PWQUALITY_CONF" | cut -d'=' -f2)
    if [[ $current_minlen -lt 14 ]]; then
        update_config_param "minlen" "14" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
    fi
else
    echo "minlen=14" >> "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
fi

# Set other password quality parameters for better security
update_config_param "dcredit" "-1" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"  # At least 1 digit required
update_config_param "ucredit" "-1" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"  # At least 1 uppercase required
update_config_param "lcredit" "-1" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"  # At least 1 lowercase required
update_config_param "ocredit" "-1" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"  # At least 1 special character required
update_config_param "maxrepeat" "3" "$PWQUALITY_CONF" # Max 3 repeated characters
update_config_param "usercheck" "1" "$PWQUALITY_CONF" # Check against user name

echo "pwquality.conf configuration complete"

# Configure PAM for password changes (system-auth and password-auth)
echo "Configuring PAM modules..."

SYSTEM_AUTH="/etc/pam.d/system-auth"
if [[ -f "$SYSTEM_AUTH" ]]; then

        sed -i '/^password.*pam_unix\.so/i password    requisite     pam_pwquality.so try_first_pass local_users_only' "$SYSTEM_AUTH"
        echo "Added pam_pwquality.so to $SYSTEM_AUTH"
    else
        # Ensure it has retry=3 option for better UX
        sed -i 's/pam_pwquality\.so.*/pam_pwquality.so try_first_pass local_users_only retry=3/' "$SYSTEM_AUTH"
    fi
fi

# Update password-auth
PASSWORD_AUTH="/etc/pam.d/password-auth"
if [[ -f "$PASSWORD_AUTH" ]]; then
    if ! grep -q "pam_pwquality.so" "$PASSWORD_AUTH"; then
        sed -i '/^password.*pam_unix\.so/i password    requisite     pam_pwquality.so try_first_pass local_users_only' "$PASSWORD_AUTH"
        echo "Added pam_pwquality.so to $PASSWORD_AUTH"
    else
        sed -i 's/pam_pwquality\.so.*/pam_pwquality.so try_first_pass local_users_only retry=3/' "$PASSWORD_AUTH"
    fi
fi

echo "PAM configuration complete"

# Verify the configuration
echo ""
echo "Verification of password quality policy configuration:"
echo "======================================================"
echo ""
echo "pwquality.conf settings:"
grep -E "^(minclass|minlen|dcredit|ucredit|lcredit|ocredit|maxrepeat|usercheck)" "$PWQUALITY_CONF" || true

echo ""
echo "PAM pwquality module status in system-auth:"
grep "pam_pwquality" "$SYSTEM_AUTH" || echo "Not configured"

echo ""
echo "PAM pwquality module status in password-auth:"
grep "pam_pwquality" "$PASSWORD_AUTH" || echo "Not configured"

echo ""
echo "Password quality policy strengthening complete!"
echo "New passwords must now contain characters from at least 4 categories:"
echo "  1. Uppercase letters (A-Z)"
echo "  2. Lowercase letters (a-z)"
echo "  3. Digits (0-9)"
echo "  4. Special characters (!@#$%^&*...)"
echo ""
echo "Minimum password length: 14 characters"
echo "Maximum repeated characters: 3"

#!/bin/bash

set -e

# RHEL 8 system logging service TLS encryption configuration script
# This script configures rsyslog to forward logs using TLS encryption

# Enable and start rsyslog service
systemctl enable rsyslog
systemctl start rsyslog

# Create directory for rsyslog configuration if it doesn't exist
RSYSLOG_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
mkdir -p "$RSYSLOG_CONFIG_DIR"

# Create TLS configuration file for rsyslog
TLS_CONFIG_FILE="$RSYSLOG_CONFIG_DIR/30-tls-encryption.conf"

# Check if configuration already exists and is correct
if [ -f "$TLS_CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
    # Verify the configuration contains TLS settings
    if grep -q "GnuTLSPriorityString" "$TLS_CONFIG_FILE" && \
       grep -q "DefaultNetstreamDriver" "$TLS_CONFIG_FILE" && \
       grep -q "ActionSendStreamDriver" "$TLS_CONFIG_FILE"; then
        echo "TLS encryption is already configured in rsyslog"
        exit 0
    fi
fi

# Create certificate directory if it doesn't exist
CERT_DIR="/etc/pki/rsyslog"
mkdir -p "$CERT_DIR"

# Generate self-signed certificate if it doesn't exist
CERT_FILE="$CERT_DIR/rsyslog-cert.pem"
KEY_FILE="$CERT_DIR/rsyslog-key.pem"

if [ ! -f "$CERT_FILE" ] || [ ! -f "$KEY_FILE" ]; then
    # Generate private key and certificate
    openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -keyout "$KEY_FILE" -out "$CERT_FILE" \
        -days 3650 -nodes -subj "/CN=localhost" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Set proper permissions
    chmod 600 "$KEY_FILE"
    chmod 644 "$CERT_FILE"
fi

# Create the TLS configuration file for rsyslog
cat > "$TLS_CONFIG_FILE" << 'EOF'
# TLS Encryption Configuration for Rsyslog
# This ensures all forwarded log records are encrypted using TLS

# Load necessary modules
module(load="imtcp")
module(load="omtcp")

# Set default netstream driver to use TLS
$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls

# Set GnuTLS priority string for strong encryption
$ActionSendStreamDriverMode 1
$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name
$ActionSendStreamDriver gtls

# Configure GnuTLS priority to ensure strong encryption
$GnuTLSPriorityString "SECURE128:+SECURE192:-VERS-ALL:+VERS-TLS1.2:+VERS-TLS1.3"

# Set CA certificate path (optional, for certificate verification)
# $DefaultNetstreamDriverCAFile /etc/pki/rsyslog/ca-cert.pem

# Set certificate file for client authentication
# $DefaultNetstreamDriverCertFile /etc/pki/rsyslog/rsyslog-cert.pem
# $DefaultNetstreamDriverKeyFile /etc/pki/rsyslog/rsyslog-key.pem

# Forward all logs to remote syslog server using TLS encryption
# Replace SYSLOG_SERVER with your actual syslog server hostname or IP
# Replace 10514 with your actual syslog server TLS port if different
# action(type="omtcp" Target="SYSLOG_SERVER" Port="10514" StreamDriver="gtls" StreamDriverMode="1" StreamDriverAuthMode="x509/name")

EOF

# Verify rsyslog syntax
if rsyslogd -N1 > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Rsyslog configuration syntax is valid"
else
    echo "Warning: Rsyslog configuration syntax check failed"
    exit 1
fi

# Set proper permissions on the configuration directory
chmod 755 "$RSYSLOG_CONFIG_DIR"
chmod 644 "$TLS_CONFIG_FILE"

# Restart rsyslog to apply the configuration
systemctl restart rsyslog

# Verify rsyslog is running
if systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog; then
    echo "Rsyslog service is running with TLS encryption configured"
else
    echo "Error: Rsyslog service failed to start"
    exit 1
fi

# Create a systemd drop-in directory for rsyslog to ensure persistence
SYSTEMD_DROPDIR="/etc/systemd/system/rsyslog.service.d"
mkdir -p "$SYSTEMD_DROPDIR"

# Create drop-in file to ensure rsyslog starts after network is available
cat > "$SYSTEMD_DROPDIR/override.conf" << 'EOF'
[Unit]
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=10
EOF

# Reload systemd configuration
systemctl daemon-reload

# Enable and restart rsyslog to apply systemd changes
systemctl enable rsyslog
systemctl restart rsyslog

# Final verification
if grep -q "DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls" "$TLS_CONFIG_FILE"; then
    echo "TLS encryption for log forwarding has been successfully configured and will persist across reboots"
    exit 0
else
    echo "Error: TLS configuration was not properly applied"
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Script to ensure SSH server displays last login information on RHEL 8
# This is an idempotent script that configures SSH to show the last successful login time

set -e

SSH_CONFIG_FILE="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
BACKUP_FILE="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.backup.$(date +%s)"

# Check if the system is RHEL 8
if ! grep -q "Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8" /etc/os-release 2>/dev/null && \
   ! grep -q "CentOS Linux 8" /etc/os-release 2>/dev/null && \
   ! grep -q "AlmaLinux" /etc/os-release 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Warning: This system does not appear to be RHEL 8. Proceeding anyway..."
fi

# Create a backup of the original sshd_config
if [ -f "$SSH_CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
    if [ ! -f "${SSH_CONFIG_FILE}.original" ]; then
        cp "$SSH_CONFIG_FILE" "${SSH_CONFIG_FILE}.original"
    fi
fi

# Function to check if a configuration line exists and is uncommented
check_config_exists() {
    grep -q "^[[:space:]]*$1[[:space:]]" "$SSH_CONFIG_FILE"
}

# Function to add or update a configuration parameter
set_config_parameter() {
    local parameter=$1
    local value=$2
    local config_file=$3
    
    if grep -q "^#.*[[:space:]]$parameter[[:space:]]" "$config_file"; then
        # Found commented out version - uncomment and set value
        sed -i "s/^#[[:space:]]*$parameter[[:space:]].*/PrintLastLog yes/" "$config_file"
    elif grep -q "^[[:space:]]*$parameter[[:space:]]" "$config_file"; then
        # Already uncommented - update the value
        sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*$parameter[[:space:]].*/PrintLastLog yes/" "$config_file"
    else
        # Parameter doesn't exist - add it
        echo "$parameter $value" >> "$config_file"
    fi
}

# Enable PrintLastLog in SSH configuration
# PrintLastLog causes sshd to print the date and time when the user last logged in
echo "Configuring SSH to display last login information..."

# Ensure PrintLastLog is set to yes
if grep -q "^[[:space:]]*PrintLastLog" "$SSH_CONFIG_FILE"; then
    # Parameter exists, update it to yes
    sed -i 's/^[[:space:]]*PrintLastLog[[:space:]].*/PrintLastLog yes/' "$SSH_CONFIG_FILE"
else
    # Parameter doesn't exist, add it
    echo "PrintLastLog yes" >> "$SSH_CONFIG_FILE"
fi

# Verify the configuration syntax
if ! sshd -t > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: SSH configuration syntax check failed. Restoring backup..."
    if [ -f "$BACKUP_FILE" ]; then
        cp "$BACKUP_FILE" "$SSH_CONFIG_FILE"
    fi
    exit 1
fi

# Reload SSH service to apply changes
echo "Reloading SSH service..."
systemctl reload sshd

# Verify the configuration is correct
if grep -q "^PrintLastLog yes" "$SSH_CONFIG_FILE"; then
    echo "SUCCESS: SSH server is now configured to display last login information."
    echo "Users will see the date and time of their last successful login on subsequent authentications."
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to configure SSH server properly."
    exit 1
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# This script configures audit rules for directory deletion tracking on RHEL 8
# It ensures idempotent operation and persistence across reboots

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/deletion_tracking.rules"
AUDIT_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"

# Ensure audit daemon is installed
if ! command -v auditd &> /dev/null; then
    echo "Installing audit daemon..."
    yum install -y audit audit-libs
fi

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
mkdir -p "$AUDIT_CONFIG_DIR"

# Function to get all available architectures
get_architectures() {
    local archs=()
    
    # Check current system architecture
    local current_arch=$(uname -m)
    archs+=("$current_arch")
    
    # For 64-bit systems, also add 32-bit if available
    if [[ "$current_arch" == "x86_64" ]]; then
        archs+=("i686")
    fi
    
    printf '%s\n' "${archs[@]}"
}

# Create the audit rules file content
create_audit_rules() {
    local rules_content=""
    
    # Add header comment
    rules_content+="# Audit rules for tracking directory deletion activity\n"
    rules_content+="# Captures rmdir operations by non-system users (UID >= 1000)\n"
    rules_content+="# Generated for RHEL 8 compliance\n\n"
    
    # Get available architectures
    local architectures=($(get_architectures | sort -u))
    
    # Add deletion tracking rules for each architecture
    for arch in "${architectures[@]}"; do
        # Rules for rmdir syscall (directory removal)
        rules_content+="-a always,exit -F arch=$arch -S rmdir -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -F key=deletion_tracking\n"
        
        # Rules for unlink and unlinkat syscalls (file/directory removal)
        rules_content+="-a always,exit -F arch=$arch -S unlink -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -F key=deletion_tracking\n"
        rules_content+="-a always,exit -F arch=$arch -S unlinkat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -F key=deletion_tracking\n"
        
        # Rules for rename/renameat (can be used to remove via renaming)
        rules_content+="-a always,exit -F arch=$arch -S rename -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -F key=deletion_tracking\n"
        rules_content+="-a always,exit -F arch=$arch -S renameat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -F key=deletion_tracking\n"
    done
    
    # Add make rules immutable at the end
    rules_content+="\n# Make the configuration immutable\n"
    rules_content+="-e 2\n"
    
    echo -e "$rules_content"
}

# Check if rules already exist and are current
check_existing_rules() {
    if [[ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]]; then
        # Check if the file contains our deletion_tracking key
        if grep -q "key=deletion_tracking" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"; then
            return 0  # Rules already exist
        fi
    fi
    return 1  # Rules don't exist or are incomplete
}

# Backup existing rules if they exist
backup_existing_rules() {
    if [[ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]]; then
        local timestamp=$(date +%s)
        cp "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}.backup.${timestamp}"
        echo "Backed up existing rules to ${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}.backup.${timestamp}"
    fi
}

# Main remediation function
main() {
    echo "Configuring audit rules for directory deletion tracking..."
    
    # Check if rules already exist
    if check_existing_rules; then
        echo "Deletion tracking rules already configured. Verifying..."
    else
        echo "Creating new deletion tracking rules..."
        backup_existing_rules
    fi
    
    # Write the audit rules file
    create_audit_rules > "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    chmod 640 "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    
    echo "Audit rules file created at: $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    
    # Load the audit rules
    echo "Loading audit rules..."
    if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
        # Audit daemon is running, reload the rules
        augenrules --load
        auditctl -R "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" || true
    else
        # Start audit daemon
        echo "Starting audit daemon..."
        systemctl start auditd
        systemctl enable auditd
    fi
    
    # Verify rules were loaded
    echo "Verifying loaded rules..."
    auditctl -l | grep -c "deletion_tracking" > /dev/null && echo "✓ Deletion tracking rules verified" || echo "⚠ Warning: Could not verify all rules"
    
    # Ensure auditd persists across reboots
    systemctl enable auditd
    
    echo "Configuration complete. Deletion tracking is now active."
    
    # Test the configuration
    echo ""
    echo "Testing audit configuration..."
    auditctl -l | grep "deletion_tracking" | head -3

main

# This script configures audit rules for directory deletion tracking on RHEL 8

    yum install -y audit audit-libs
fi

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
mkdir -p "$AUDIT_CONFIG_DIR"

# Function to get all available architectures
get_architectures() {
    local archs=()
    
    # Check current system architecture
    local current_arch=$(uname -m)
    archs+=("$current_arch")
    
    # For 64-bit systems, also add 32-bit if available
    if [[ "$current_arch" == "x86_64" ]]; then
        archs+=("i686")
    fi
    
    printf '%s\n' "${archs[@]}"
}

# Create the audit rules file content
create_audit_rules() {
    local rules_content=""
    
    # Add header comment
    rules_content+="# Audit rules for tracking directory deletion activity\n"
    rules_content+="# Captures rmdir operations by non-system users (UID >= 1000)\n"
    rules_content+="# Generated for RHEL 8 compliance\n\n"
    
    # Get available architectures
    local architectures=($(get_architectures | sort -u))

    done

}

# Check if rules already exist and are current

}

    fi
}

        echo "$AUDIT_RULE" >> /etc/audit/rules.d/privileged.rules
    fi

        auditctl -a exit,always -F path=/usr/sbin/usermod -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k privileged 2>/dev/null || true
    fi

# Ensure auditd reloads rules at startup by checking both rule loading mechanisms

if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
    augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || true

        exit 0
    else

    fi
fi

# This script configures audit rules to record all write and attribute-change events
# affecting the secure group-account database on RHEL 8 systems.

if ! command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y audit audit-libs
fi

systemctl enable auditd
systemctl start auditd

# Define the audit rules for group account database
# /etc/gshadow is the secure group-account database
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/group_account_modification.rules"

# Create the audit rules file if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]; then
    cat > "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" << 'EOF'
# Audit rule for secure group-account database modifications
# Tagged with "group_account_modifications" for grouping related events
-w /etc/gshadow -p wa -k group_account_modifications
-w /etc/group -p wa -k group_account_modifications
EOF
    chmod 640 "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
fi

    cat >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" << 'EOF'
# Audit rule for secure group-account database modifications
# Tagged with "group_account_modifications" for grouping related events
-w /etc/gshadow -p wa -k group_account_modifications
-w /etc/group -p wa -k group_account_modifications
EOF
fi

# Load the rules using augenrules (rules-compilation mechanism)
# This ensures persistence across reboots
if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
    augenrules --load
else
    # Fallback to auditctl (direct-load utility) if augenrules is not available
    auditctl -w /etc/gshadow -p wa -k group_account_modifications
    auditctl -w /etc/group -p wa -k group_account_modifications
fi

[Service]
ExecStartPost=/sbin/augenrules --load
EOF

fi

# Verify the audit rules are loaded
auditctl -l | grep -q "group_account_modifications" || {
    echo "Warning: Audit rules may not be loaded properly"
    exit 1
}

echo "Audit rules for group account modifications have been configured successfully."

# Prevent ATM kernel module from loading

    echo "Warning: This system may not be RHEL 8"

if [ -f "$MODPROBE_CONFIG" ]; then
    if grep -q "^install atm /bin/false" "$MODPROBE_CONFIG" && grep -q "^blacklist atm" "$MODPROBE_CONFIG"; then

# Disable ATM kernel module
install atm /bin/false
blacklist atm
EOF

# Unload the module if it's currently loaded

    echo "Attempting to unload currently loaded ATM module..."

if ! grep -q "release 8" /etc/redhat-release 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Warning: This system may not be RHEL 8"
fi

# Create modprobe configuration to block ATM module
MODPROBE_CONFIG="/etc/modprobe.d/atm-disable.conf"

# Check if configuration file already exists with the correct content
if [ -f "$MODPROBE_CONFIG" ]; then
    if grep -q "^install atm /bin/false" "$MODPROBE_CONFIG" && grep -q "^blacklist atm" "$MODPROBE_CONFIG"; then
        echo "ATM module is already disabled"
        exit 0
    fi
fi

# Create the modprobe configuration file
cat > "$MODPROBE_CONFIG" << 'EOF'
# Disable ATM kernel module
install atm /bin/false
blacklist atm
EOF

chmod 644 "$MODPROBE_CONFIG"
echo "ATM module blocking configured in $MODPROBE_CONFIG"

# Unload the module if it's currently loaded
if lsmod | grep -q "^atm "; then
    echo "Attempting to unload currently loaded ATM module..."
    modprobe -r atm 2>/dev/null || echo "Note: ATM module could not be unloaded (may be in use)"
fi

echo "ATM kernel module has been blocked from loading"
```

#!/bin/bash

# Script to configure audit rules for SSH authentication agent (ssh-agent) execution
# This script is idempotent and works with both auditctl and audit.rules mechanisms

set -e

# SSH agent program path
SSH_AGENT_PATH="/usr/bin/ssh-agent"

# Check if ssh-agent exists
if [ ! -f "$SSH_AGENT_PATH" ]; then
    echo "Error: SSH agent not found at $SSH_AGENT_PATH"
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure auditd is installed
if ! command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
    echo "Installing audit daemon..."
    yum install -y audit audit-libs
fi

# Function to check if a rule already exists
rule_exists() {
    local rule="$1"
    auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -F "$rule" > /dev/null 2>&1
    return $?
}

# Function to add rule via auditctl
add_rule_via_auditctl() {
    local rule="$1"
    if rule_exists "$rule"; then
        echo "Rule already exists: $rule"
        return 0
    fi
    
    echo "Adding audit rule: $rule"
    auditctl "$rule" || {
        echo "Failed to add rule via auditctl"
        return 1
    }
}

# The audit rule for ssh-agent execution
# -a: append rule to end of list
# always: always create a record
# -F arch=b64: for 64-bit systems (also add b32 for 32-bit)
# -S execve: system call for execution
# -F exe=/usr/bin/ssh-agent: only for ssh-agent executable
# -F auid>=1000: regular users (UID >= 1000) or root
# -F auid!=-1: exclude unset login UID
# -k: tag with key for identification

# Create rules array for both 64-bit and 32-bit systems
declare -a RULES
RULES=(
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S execve -F exe=$SSH_AGENT_PATH -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k privileged-ssh-agent"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S execve -F exe=$SSH_AGENT_PATH -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k privileged-ssh-agent"
)

# Add rule for root execution (auid=0)
RULES+=(
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S execve -F exe=$SSH_AGENT_PATH -F auid=0 -k privileged-ssh-agent"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S execve -F exe=$SSH_AGENT_PATH -F auid=0 -k privileged-ssh-agent"
)

# Determine which audit rules file to use
AUDIT_RULES_D_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
AUDIT_RULES_TARGET=""

# Check if using rules.d directory (preferred method in RHEL 8)
if [ -d "$AUDIT_RULES_D_DIR" ]; then
    AUDIT_RULES_TARGET="$AUDIT_RULES_D_DIR/ssh-agent.rules"
else
    AUDIT_RULES_TARGET="$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
fi

# Function to add rules to audit configuration file
add_rules_to_file() {
    local target_file="$1"
    shift
    local rules=("$@")
    
    # Create rules.d directory if it doesn't exist
    if [[ "$target_file" == "$AUDIT_RULES_D_DIR"* ]]; then
        mkdir -p "$AUDIT_RULES_D_DIR"
    fi
    
    # Check if file exists and contains any of our rules
    local rules_added=0
    
    for rule in "${rules[@]}"; do
        if [ -f "$target_file" ]; then
            # Check if this specific rule already exists
            if grep -F "$rule" "$target_file" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
                echo "Rule already in $target_file: $rule"
                continue
            fi
        fi
        
        echo "Adding rule to $target_file: $rule"
        echo "$rule" >> "$target_file"
        rules_added=$((rules_added + 1))
    done
    
    return $((rules_added > 0 ? 0 : 1))
}

# Add rules to configuration file
if add_rules_to_file "$AUDIT_RULES_TARGET" "${RULES[@]}"; then
    echo "Rules added to $AUDIT_RULES_TARGET"
else
    echo "All rules already present in $AUDIT_RULES_TARGET"
fi

# Load rules into active audit daemon
# First check if auditd is running
if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
    echo "Reloading audit rules..."
    # Load rules via auditctl for immediate effect
    for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
        if ! auditctl $rule 2>/dev/null; then
            # If the rule already exists, auditctl might report an error but it's ok
            # Check if it was added previously
            if ! rule_exists "$rule"; then
                echo "Warning: Failed to load rule: $rule"
            fi
        fi
    done
    
    # Save the current state
    auditctl -w /etc/audit/rules.d/ -p wa -k audit-rules-modification
else
    echo "Audit daemon not running. Rules will be loaded on next audit daemon startup."
fi

# Enable and start auditd service
echo "Enabling audit daemon..."
systemctl enable auditd

if ! systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
    echo "Starting audit daemon..."
    systemctl start auditd
fi

# Verify the rules are loaded
echo ""
echo "Verifying audit rules for ssh-agent:"
auditctl -l | grep -E "ssh-agent|privileged-ssh-agent" || echo "Checking rule file..."

# Display the contents of the rule file
echo ""
echo "Rule file contents ($AUDIT_RULES_TARGET):"
if [ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_TARGET" ]; then
    grep -E "ssh-agent|privileged-ssh-agent" "$AUDIT_RULES_TARGET" || echo "No ssh-agent rules found in file (will be loaded on daemon restart)"
else
    echo "Rule file not found: $AUDIT_RULES_TARGET"
fi

echo ""
echo "Audit configuration complete. SSH-agent execution will be logged with key 'privileged-ssh-agent'"
echo "Log entries can be searched with: ausearch -k privileged-ssh-agent"

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# RHEL 8 Audit Configuration Script for DAC Permission Changes
# This script configures the audit subsystem to track chmod operations by regular users

set -e

# Define the audit rule to be added
# The rule monitors chmod operations for regular (non-system) users
# -a always,exit: Always record on exit
# -F arch=b32/b64: Monitor both 32-bit and 64-bit syscalls
# -S chmod,fchmod,fchmodat: Monitor all chmod-related syscalls
# -F auid>=1000: Only track regular users (auid >= 1000), excluding unset auid (-F auid!=-1)
# -F auid!=-1: Exclude unset login UIDs
# -k perm_mod: Tag the records with 'perm_mod' for easy retrieval

AUDIT_RULE_32="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S chmod,fchmod,fchmodat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k perm_mod"
AUDIT_RULE_64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S chmod,fchmod,fchmodat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k perm_mod"

# Directory for audit rules
AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/perm_mod.rules"

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
if [ ! -d "$AUDIT_RULES_DIR" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$AUDIT_RULES_DIR"
fi

# Function to check if a rule exists in the file
rule_exists() {
    local rule="$1"
    grep -q "^${rule}$" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null
}

# Function to add audit rule idempotently
add_audit_rule() {
    local rule="$1"
    
    if ! rule_exists "$rule"; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
        echo "Added audit rule: $rule"
    else
        echo "Audit rule already exists: $rule"
    fi
}

# Create or update the audit rules file
if [ ! -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]; then
    touch "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    echo "Created audit rules file: $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
fi

# Add both 32-bit and 64-bit rules
add_audit_rule "$AUDIT_RULE_32"
add_audit_rule "$AUDIT_RULE_64"

# Set appropriate permissions on the rules file
chmod 640 "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# Load the new audit rules
# First, check if auditctl is available
if command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
    # Load the rules from the file
    auditctl -R "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    
    # Make the rules persistent across reboots
    # This ensures the rules are reloaded when the audit daemon starts
    systemctl restart auditd
    
    echo "Audit rules loaded and audit daemon restarted"
else
    echo "Warning: auditctl not found. Audit daemon may not be installed."
fi

# Verify the rules are loaded
if command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
    echo "Verifying loaded audit rules:"
    auditctl -l | grep "perm_mod" || echo "Warning: perm_mod rules may not be loaded"
fi

echo "Audit configuration completed successfully"

#!/bin/bash

# RHEL 8 GNOME SmartCard Lock Configuration Script
# This script configures mandatory system-wide policies to lock the screen
# when a smartcard is removed, preventing unauthorized access to unattended sessions

set -e

# Color codes for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Function to print colored output
log_info() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}[INFO]${NC} $1"
}

log_error() {
    echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"
}

log_warn() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"
}

# Function to check if script is running as root
check_root() {
    if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
        log_error "This script must be run as root"
        exit 1
    fi
}

# Function to check if RHEL 8
check_rhel8() {
    if ! grep -qi "release 8" /etc/redhat-release 2>/dev/null; then
        log_warn "This script is optimized for RHEL 8. Current system may not be RHEL 8."
    fi
}

# Function to check if GNOME is installed
check_gnome() {
    if ! command -v gnome-shell &> /dev/null; then
        log_error "GNOME Shell is not installed"
        exit 1
    fi
    log_info "GNOME Shell found"
}

# Function to install required packages
install_dependencies() {
    log_info "Checking and installing required dependencies..."
    
    # Check if pcsc-lite is installed (smartcard support)
    if ! rpm -q pcsc-lite &>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Installing pcsc-lite for smartcard support..."
        yum install -y pcsc-lite pcsc-lite-libs &>/dev/null
    fi
    
    # Check if GNOME settings tools are installed
    if ! rpm -q gsettings-desktop-schemas &>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Installing gsettings-desktop-schemas..."
        yum install -y gsettings-desktop-schemas &>/dev/null
    fi
    
    # Check if dconf is installed
    if ! command -v dconf &> /dev/null; then
        log_info "Installing dconf..."
        yum install -y dconf &>/dev/null
    fi
    
    log_info "Dependencies check completed"
}

# Function to configure smartcard lock behavior via dconf
configure_dconf_settings() {
    log_info "Configuring GNOME dconf settings for smartcard lock..."
    
    # Create dconf database directory if it doesn't exist
    mkdir -p /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks
    mkdir -p /etc/dconf/profile
    
    # Create/update the dconf profile
    cat > /etc/dconf/profile/user << 'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF
    
    # Create the local dconf database with smartcard settings
    cat > /etc/dconf/db/local.d/01-smartcard-lock << 'EOF'
# Smartcard removal lock settings for GNOME
[org/gnome/desktop/session]
idle-delay=uint32 300
session-timeout=uint32 600

[org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard]
# Lock screen on smartcard removal
removal-action='lock-screen'

[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
# Enable lock on smartcard removal
lock-enabled=true
EOF
    
    # Lock the settings so users cannot override them
    cat > /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks/smartcard-locks << 'EOF'
# Lock smartcard settings to prevent user override
/org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard/removal-action
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-enabled
/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay
/org/gnome/desktop/session/session-timeout
EOF
    
    log_info "dconf settings created"
}

# Function to compile dconf database
compile_dconf_database() {
    log_info "Compiling dconf database..."
    
    # Compile the dconf database
    dconf update 2>/dev/null || {
        log_warn "dconf update had issues, but continuing..."
    }
    
    log_info "dconf database compiled"
}

# Function to configure PAM for smartcard support
configure_pam_smartcard() {
    log_info "Configuring PAM for smartcard support..."
    
    # Check if pam_pkcs11 is installed
    if ! rpm -q pam_pkcs11 &>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Installing pam_pkcs11..."
        yum install -y pam_pkcs11 &>/dev/null || {
            log_warn "pam_pkcs11 installation failed, but continuing with dconf settings..."
        }
    fi
    
    log_info "PAM smartcard configuration checked"
}

# Function to enable pcscd service
enable_pcscd_service() {
    log_info "Enabling and starting pcscd service..."
    
    # Enable pcscd to start at boot
    systemctl enable pcscd 2>/dev/null || {
        log_warn "Failed to enable pcscd, but continuing..."
    }
    
    # Start pcscd if not running
    systemctl start pcscd 2>/dev/null || {
        log_warn "Failed to start pcscd, but continuing..."
    }
    
    log_info "pcscd service configuration completed"
}

# Function to create GNOME policy file
create_gnome_policy() {
    log_info "Creating GNOME mandatory policy file..."
    
    mkdir -p /etc/polkit-1/rules.d
    
    cat > /etc/polkit-1/rules.d/99-smartcard-lock.rules << 'EOF'
polkit.addRule(function(action, subject) {
    // Prevent users from disabling smartcard lock
    if (action.id == "org.gnome.settings-daemon.peripherals.smartcard" ||
        action.id == "org.gnome.desktop.screensaver") {
        if (subject.isInGroup("wheel")) {
            return polkit.Result.YES;
        }
        return polkit.Result.NO;
    }
});
EOF
    
    log_info "GNOME policy file created"
}

# Function to create systemd service for smartcard monitoring
create_smartcard_monitor_service() {
    log_info "Creating smartcard monitoring systemd service..."
    
    cat > /etc/systemd/system/smartcard-lock.service << 'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=Lock screen on smartcard removal
After=pcscd.service
Wants=pcscd.service

[Service]
Type=simple

#!/bin/bash

# Monitor smartcard status and lock screen on removal
# This script runs as a systemd service

LAST_STATUS=""

monitor_smartcard() {
    while true; do
        # Check if smartcard reader is available
        if command -v pcsc_scan &> /dev/null; then
            # Use pcsc_scan to check smartcard status (non-interactive)
            CURRENT_STATUS=$(timeout 2 pcsc_scan -n 2>/dev/null | grep -i "card state" | head -1)
        else
            # Fallback: check if pcscd is running
            CURRENT_STATUS=$(systemctl is-active pcscd 2>/dev/null)
        fi
        
        # If status changed and no card is present, lock the screen
        if [ -n "$LAST_STATUS" ] && [ "$CURRENT_STATUS" != "$LAST_STATUS" ]; then
            if echo "$CURRENT_STATUS" | grep -qi "absent\|removed"; then
                # Notify all active GNOME sessions to lock
                for user in $(who | awk '{print $1}' | sort -u); do
                    USER_ID=$(id -u "$user" 2>/dev/null)
                    if [ -n "$USER_ID" ]; then
                        DBUS_ADDRESS="unix:path=/run/user/$USER_ID/bus"
                        DISPLAY=":0"
                        # Use dbus to signal screen lock
                        sudo -u "$user" DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS="$DBUS_ADDRESS" \
                            dbus-send --print-reply --dest=org.gnome.ScreenSaver \
                            /org/gnome/ScreenSaver org.gnome.ScreenSaver.Lock 2>/dev/null || true
                    fi
                done
            fi
        fi
        
        LAST_STATUS="$CURRENT_STATUS"
        sleep 5
    done
}

monitor_smartcard

#!/bin/bash

# RHEL 8 GNOME SmartCard Lock Configuration Script
# This script configures mandatory system-wide policies to lock the screen
# when a smartcard is removed, preventing unauthorized access to unattended sessions

set -e

# Color codes for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Function to print colored output
log_info() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}[INFO]${NC} $1"
}

log_error() {
    echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"
}

log_warn() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"
}

# Function to check if script is running as root
check_root() {
    if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
        log_error "This script must be run as root"
        exit 1
    fi
}

# Function to check if RHEL 8
check_rhel8() {
    if ! grep -qi "release 8" /etc/redhat-release 2>/dev/null; then
        log_warn "This script is optimized for RHEL 8. Current system may not be RHEL 8."
    fi
}

# Function to check if GNOME is installed
check_gnome() {
    if ! command -v gnome-shell &> /dev/null; then
        log_error "GNOME Shell is not installed"
        exit 1
    fi
    log_info "GNOME Shell found"
}

# Function to install required packages
install_dependencies() {
    log_info "Checking and installing required dependencies..."
    
    # Check if pcsc-lite is installed (smartcard support)
    if ! rpm -q pcsc-lite &>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Installing pcsc-lite for smartcard support..."
        yum install -y pcsc-lite pcsc-lite-libs &>/dev/null
    fi
    
    # Check if GNOME settings tools are installed
    if ! rpm -q gsettings-desktop-schemas &>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Installing gsettings-desktop-schemas..."
        yum install -y gsettings-desktop-schemas &>/dev/null
    fi
    
    # Check if dconf is installed
    if ! command -v dconf &> /dev/null; then
        log_info "Installing dconf..."
        yum install -y dconf &>/dev/null
    fi
    
    log_info "Dependencies check completed"
}

# Function to configure smartcard lock behavior via dconf
configure_dconf_settings() {
    log_info "Configuring GNOME dconf settings for smartcard lock..."
    
    # Create dconf database directory if it doesn't exist
    mkdir -p /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks
    mkdir -p /etc/dconf/profile
    
    # Create/update the dconf profile
    cat > /etc/dconf/profile/user << 'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF
    
    # Create the local dconf database with smartcard settings
    cat > /etc/dconf/db/local.d/01-smartcard-lock << 'EOF'
# Smartcard removal lock settings for GNOME
[org/gnome/desktop/session]
idle-delay=uint32 300
session-timeout=uint32 600

[org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard]
# Lock screen on smartcard removal
removal-action='lock-screen'

[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
# Enable lock on smartcard removal
lock-enabled=true
EOF
    
    # Lock the settings so users cannot override them
    cat > /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks/smartcard-locks << 'EOF'
# Lock smartcard settings to prevent user override
/org/gnome/settings-daemon/peripherals/smartcard/removal-action
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-enabled
/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay
/org/gnome/desktop/session/session-timeout
EOF
    
    log_info "dconf settings created"
}

# Function to compile dconf database
compile_dconf_database() {
    log_info "Compiling dconf database..."
    
    # Compile the dconf database
    dconf update 2>/dev/null || {
        log_warn "dconf update had issues, but continuing..."
    }
    
    log_info "dconf database compiled"
}

# Function to configure PAM for smartcard support
configure_pam_smartcard() {
    log_info "Configuring PAM for smartcard support..."
    
    # Check if pam_pkcs11 is installed
    if ! rpm -q pam_pkcs11 &>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Installing pam_pkcs11..."
        yum install -y pam_pkcs11 &>/dev/null || {
            log_warn "pam_pkcs11 installation failed, but continuing with dconf settings..."
        }
    fi
    
    log_info "PAM smartcard configuration checked"
}

# Function to enable pcscd service
enable_pcscd_service() {
    log_info "Enabling and starting pcscd service..."
    
    # Enable pcscd to start at boot
    systemctl enable pcscd 2>/dev/null || {
        log_warn "Failed to enable pcscd, but continuing..."
    }
    
    # Start pcscd if not running
    systemctl start pcscd 2>/dev/null || {
        log_warn "Failed to start pcscd, but continuing..."
    }
    
    log_info "pcscd service configuration completed"
}

# Function to create GNOME policy file
create_gnome_policy() {
    log_info "Creating GNOME mandatory policy file..."
    
    mkdir -p /etc/polkit-1/rules.d
    
    cat > /etc/polkit-1/rules.d/99-smartcard-lock.rules << 'EOF'
polkit.addRule(function(action, subject) {
    // Prevent users from disabling smartcard lock
    if (action.id == "org.gnome.settings-daemon.peripherals.smartcard" ||
        action.id == "org.gnome.desktop.screensaver") {
        if (subject.isInGroup("wheel")) {
            return polkit.Result.YES;
        }
        return polkit.Result.NO;
    }
});
EOF
    
    log_info "GNOME policy file created"
}

# Function to create systemd service for smartcard monitoring
create_smartcard_monitor_service() {
    log_info "Creating smartcard monitoring systemd service..."
    
    cat > /etc/systemd/system/smartcard-lock.service << 'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=Lock screen on smartcard removal
After=pcscd.service
Wants=pcscd.service

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/smartcard-monitor.sh
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target graphical.target
EOF
    
    # Create the monitoring script
    cat > /usr/local/bin/smartcard-monitor.sh << 'SCRIPT'

        fi
        
        LAST_STATUS="$CURRENT_STATUS"

#!/bin/bash

# Script to configure RHEL 8 system logging for authentication, privilege-authentication, and daemon activity
# This script ensures persistent logging across reboots

set -e

# Function to log messages
log_message() {
    echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $*"
}

# Function to check if rsyslog is installed and running
check_rsyslog() {
    if ! command -v rsyslog &> /dev/null; then
        log_message "ERROR: rsyslog is not installed"
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

# Function to ensure rsyslog service is enabled and running
enable_rsyslog_service() {
    log_message "Enabling and starting rsyslog service..."
    systemctl enable rsyslog
    systemctl start rsyslog
    systemctl status rsyslog > /dev/null 2>&1 || {
        log_message "ERROR: Failed to start rsyslog service"
        return 1
    }
    log_message "rsyslog service is enabled and running"
}

# Function to create or update rsyslog configuration
configure_rsyslog() {
    local rsyslog_conf_dir="/etc/rsyslog.d"
    local auth_config="${rsyslog_conf_dir}/30-auth-messages.conf"
    local daemon_config="${rsyslog_conf_dir}/40-daemon-messages.conf"
    
    log_message "Configuring rsyslog for authentication and daemon logging..."
    
    # Create rsyslog.d directory if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -d "$rsyslog_conf_dir" ]; then
        mkdir -p "$rsyslog_conf_dir"
        log_message "Created $rsyslog_conf_dir directory"
    fi
    
    # Create authentication and privilege-authentication logging configuration
    if [ ! -f "$auth_config" ] || ! grep -q "auth,authpriv" "$auth_config" 2>/dev/null; then
        cat > "$auth_config" << 'EOF'
# Authentication messages configuration
auth,authpriv.*                                                 /var/log/secure
EOF
        log_message "Created/updated authentication logging configuration: $auth_config"
    else
        log_message "Authentication logging configuration already exists: $auth_config"
    fi
    
    # Create daemon messages logging configuration
    if [ ! -f "$daemon_config" ] || ! grep -q "daemon\." "$daemon_config" 2>/dev/null; then
        cat > "$daemon_config" << 'EOF'
# Daemon messages configuration
daemon.*                                                         /var/log/messages
EOF
        log_message "Created/updated daemon logging configuration: $daemon_config"
    else
        log_message "Daemon logging configuration already exists: $daemon_config"
    fi
}

# Function to ensure log files exist with proper permissions
setup_log_files() {
    local secure_log="/var/log/secure"
    local messages_log="/var/log/messages"
    
    log_message "Setting up log files with proper permissions..."
    
    # Create secure log if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "$secure_log" ]; then
        touch "$secure_log"
        chmod 600 "$secure_log"
        log_message "Created $secure_log with permissions 600"
    else
        # Ensure proper permissions
        chmod 600 "$secure_log"
        log_message "Ensured $secure_log has permissions 600"
    fi
    
    # Create messages log if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "$messages_log" ]; then
        touch "$messages_log"
        chmod 644 "$messages_log"
        log_message "Created $messages_log with permissions 644"
    else
        # Ensure proper permissions
        chmod 644 "$messages_log"
        log_message "Ensured $messages_log has permissions 644"
    fi
    
    # Set ownership
    chown root:root "$secure_log" "$messages_log"
    log_message "Set ownership of log files to root:root"
}

# Function to configure log rotation
configure_logrotate() {
    local logrotate_auth="/etc/logrotate.d/secure"
    local logrotate_messages="/etc/logrotate.d/messages"
    
    log_message "Configuring log rotation..."
    
    # Configure rotation for secure log
    if [ ! -f "$logrotate_auth" ] || ! grep -q "/var/log/secure" "$logrotate_auth" 2>/dev/null; then
        cat > "$logrotate_auth" << 'EOF'
/var/log/secure {
    daily
    rotate 7
    compress
    delaycompress
    missingok
    notifempty
    create 600 root root
    sharedscripts
    postrotate
        /bin/kill -HUP `cat /var/run/syslogd.pid 2> /dev/null` 2> /dev/null || true
    endscript
}
EOF
        log_message "Created logrotate configuration for secure log"
    else
        log_message "Logrotate configuration for secure log already exists"
    fi
    
    # Configure rotation for messages log
    if [ ! -f "$logrotate_messages" ] || ! grep -q "/var/log/messages" "$logrotate_messages" 2>/dev/null; then
        cat > "$logrotate_messages" << 'EOF'
/var/log/messages {
    daily
    rotate 7
    compress
    delaycompress
    missingok
    notifempty
    create 644 root root
    sharedscripts
    postrotate
        /bin/kill -HUP `cat /var/run/syslogd.pid 2> /dev/null` 2> /dev/null || true
    endscript
}
EOF
        log_message "Created logrotate configuration for messages log"
    else
        log_message "Logrotate configuration for messages log already exists"
    fi
}

# Function to validate rsyslog configuration
validate_rsyslog_config() {
    log_message "Validating rsyslog configuration..."
    
    if rsyslogd -N1 > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_message "rsyslog configuration is valid"
        return 0
    else
        log_message "ERROR: rsyslog configuration validation failed"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to restart rsyslog to apply configuration
restart_rsyslog() {
    log_message "Restarting rsyslog service to apply configuration..."
    
    if systemctl restart rsyslog; then
        log_message "rsyslog service restarted successfully"
        return 0
    else
        log_message "ERROR: Failed to restart rsyslog service"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to verify logging is working
verify_logging() {
    log_message "Verifying logging functionality..."
    
    # Test authentication logging by creating a test entry
    logger -t "test[$$]" -p auth.info "Test authentication message"
    
    # Give rsyslog a moment to process
    sleep 1
    
    # Check if the test message appears in secure log
    if grep -q "Test authentication message" /var/log/secure 2>/dev/null; then
        log_message "Authentication logging verification: PASSED"
    else
        log_message "WARNING: Authentication logging verification: FAILED or pending"
    fi
    
    # Test daemon logging
    logger -t "test[$$]" -p daemon.info "Test daemon message"
    
    # Give rsyslog a moment to process
    sleep 1
    
    # Check if the test message appears in messages log
    if grep -q "Test daemon message" /var/log/messages 2>/dev/null; then
        log_message "Daemon logging verification: PASSED"
    else
        log_message "WARNING: Daemon logging verification: FAILED or pending"
    fi
}

# Main execution
main() {
    log_message "Starting RHEL 8 system logging configuration..."
    
    # Check if running as root
    if [ "$EUID" -ne 0 ]; then
        log_message "ERROR: This script must be run as root"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Check for rsyslog
    if ! check_rsyslog; then
        log_message "ERROR: rsyslog is required but not found"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Configure logging
    configure_rsyslog || exit 1
    setup_log_files || exit 1
    configure_logrotate || exit 1
    
    # Validate configuration
    if ! validate_rsyslog_config; then
        log_message "ERROR: Configuration validation failed"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Enable and restart rsyslog
    enable_rsyslog_service || exit 1
    restart_rsyslog || exit 1
    
    # Verify logging is working
    verify_logging
    
    log_message "RHEL 8 system logging configuration completed successfully"
    log_message "Authentication logs: /var/log/secure"
    log_message "Daemon logs: /var/log/messages"
    log_message "Configuration will persist across reboots"
    
    exit 0
}

# Run main function
main "$@"

    verify_logging
    
    log_message "RHEL 8 system logging configuration completed successfully"
    log_message "Authentication logs: /var/log/secure"
    log_message "Daemon logs: /var/log/messages"
    log_message "Configuration will persist across reboots"
    
    exit 0
}

# Run main function
main "$@"

# RHEL 8 GNOME Screensaver Lock Delay Configuration Script
# This script ensures the screensaver locks immediately (delay = 0 seconds)

set -e

# Check if running on RHEL 8
if ! grep -q "release 8" /etc/os-release; then
    echo "This script is designed for RHEL 8 systems"
    exit 1
fi

# Check if GNOME is installed
if ! command -v gnome-shell &> /dev/null; then
    echo "GNOME is not installed on this system"
    exit 1
fi

# Set the lock delay to 0 seconds in the GNOME desktop schema
# This applies the setting system-wide
dconf update 2>/dev/null || true

# Configure the lock delay setting using dconf
# The delay-lock-on-suspend key controls the delay in seconds before locking
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-enabled true
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver delay-lock-on-suspend 0
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver delay-lock-on-suspend 0

# Also set the idle delay to ensure screensaver activates promptly
# Setting to 5 minutes (300 seconds) before screensaver engages
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 300

# For system-wide persistence, we'll also configure the dconf database defaults
# Create the necessary directories if they don't exist
mkdir -p /etc/dconf/db/gdm.d
mkdir -p /etc/dconf/profile

# Create the lock delay configuration file for system-wide settings
cat > /etc/dconf/db/gdm.d/00-lock-delay << 'EOF'
[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-enabled=true
delay-lock-on-suspend=0
idle-delay=uint32 300
EOF

# Ensure proper permissions
chmod 644 /etc/dconf/db/gdm.d/00-lock-delay

# Create or update the dconf profile to include our settings
if [ ! -f /etc/dconf/profile/gdm ]; then
    cat > /etc/dconf/profile/gdm << 'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:gdm
EOF
    chmod 644 /etc/dconf/profile/gdm
else
    # Check if the profile already has the system-db:gdm entry
    if ! grep -q "system-db:gdm" /etc/dconf/profile/gdm; then
        echo "system-db:gdm" >> /etc/dconf/profile/gdm
    fi
fi

dconf update

echo "Verifying screensaver lock delay configuration..."
lock_enabled=$(gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-enabled)
delay_seconds=$(gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.screensaver delay-lock-on-suspend)

if [ "$lock_enabled" = "true" ] && [ "$delay_seconds" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "Success: Screensaver lock delay is set to 0 seconds"
    echo "Lock enabled: $lock_enabled"
    echo "Lock delay: $delay_seconds seconds"
else
    echo "Warning: Unable to verify settings completely"
    echo "Lock enabled: $lock_enabled"
    echo "Lock delay: $delay_seconds seconds"
fi

mkdir -p /etc/skel/.config/dconf
if [ ! -f /etc/skel/.config/dconf/user ]; then
    touch /etc/skel/.config/dconf/user
fi

#!/bin/bash

# RHEL 8 Bash Remediation Script: Enforce Maximum Password Lifetime of 365 Days
# This script ensures all existing local user accounts have a maximum password lifetime
# of 365 days to prevent indefinite credential validity.

set -euo pipefail

# Define the maximum password age in days
MAX_PASS_AGE=365

# Function to get all local user accounts
get_local_users() {
    # Get all users from /etc/passwd that are not system users
    # System users typically have UIDs < 1000 on RHEL
    awk -F: '$3 >= 1000 {print $1}' /etc/passwd
}

# Function to check and update password age for a user
update_user_password_age() {
    local username="$1"
    
    # Get current maximum password age
    # chage -l returns the maximum number of days a password is valid
    local current_max_age
    current_max_age=$(chage -l "$username" 2>/dev/null | grep "Maximum number of days between password change" | awk '{print $(NF)}' || echo "")
    
    # Handle the case where the value might be -1 (unlimited) or empty
    if [[ -z "$current_max_age" ]]; then
        current_max_age="-1"
    fi
    
    # Check if update is needed (current_max_age is -1 for unlimited or greater than MAX_PASS_AGE)
    if [[ "$current_max_age" == "-1" ]] || [[ "$current_max_age" -gt "$MAX_PASS_AGE" ]]; then
        echo "Updating password maximum age for user: $username (current: $current_max_age days, setting to: $MAX_PASS_AGE days)"
        chage -M "$MAX_PASS_AGE" "$username"
        return 0
    else
        # User is already compliant
        return 1
    fi
}

# Main execution
echo "Starting remediation: Enforcing maximum password lifetime of $MAX_PASS_AGE days"
echo "========================================================================="

# Track statistics
total_users=0
updated_users=0
compliant_users=0

# Process all local users
for user in $(get_local_users); do
    ((total_users++))
    
    # Check if user is locked or has other issues that might prevent chage from working
    # We'll attempt the update and handle errors gracefully
    if update_user_password_age "$user" 2>/dev/null; then
        ((updated_users++))
    else
        ((compliant_users++))
    fi
done

echo "========================================================================="
echo "Remediation Summary:"
echo "  Total local users processed: $total_users"
echo "  Users updated to comply: $updated_users"
echo "  Users already compliant: $compliant_users"
echo ""

# Verify the changes by checking a sample of users
echo "Verification - Checking password age settings:"
for user in $(get_local_users | head -5); do
    max_age=$(chage -l "$user" 2>/dev/null | grep "Maximum number of days between password change" | awk '{print $(NF)}' || echo "ERROR")
    echo "  $user: Maximum password age = $max_age days"
done

if [[ $total_users -gt 5 ]]; then
    echo "  ... and $(($total_users - 5)) more users"
fi

echo ""
echo "Remediation completed successfully."

#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG_FILE="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
PERMIT_EMPTY_PASSWORDS_SETTING="PermitEmptyPasswords no"

check_and_configure_sshd() {
    local config_file="$1"
    local setting="$2"
    
    if ! test -f "$config_file"; then
        echo "Error: SSH config file not found at $config_file"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    grep -q "^${setting}" "$config_file" 2>/dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        current_value=$(grep "^${setting}" "$config_file" | head -1)
        if [ "$current_value" = "$setting" ]; then
            echo "SSH configuration already correct: $setting"
            return 0
        else
            sed -i "s/^PermitEmptyPasswords.*/PermitEmptyPasswords no/" "$config_file"
            echo "Updated SSH configuration to enforce: $setting"
            return 1
        fi
    else
        if grep -q "^#.*PermitEmptyPasswords" "$config_file"; then
            sed -i "s/^#.*PermitEmptyPasswords.*/PermitEmptyPasswords no/" "$config_file"
        else
            echo "$setting" >> "$config_file"
        fi
        echo "Added SSH configuration to enforce: $setting"
        return 1
    fi
}

validate_sshd_config() {
    local config_file="$1"
    
    sshd -t -f "$config_file" > /dev/null 2>&1
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "SSH configuration syntax is valid"
        return 0
    else
        echo "Error: SSH configuration has syntax errors"
        return 1
    fi
}

restart_sshd_if_needed() {
    local config_changed=$1
    
    if [ $config_changed -eq 1 ]; then
        systemctl restart sshd
        echo "SSH daemon restarted successfully"
    else
        echo "No SSH daemon restart needed"
    fi
}

echo "Starting SSH security configuration for empty password denial..."

check_and_configure_sshd "$SSHD_CONFIG_FILE" "$PERMIT_EMPTY_PASSWORDS_SETTING"
CONFIG_CHANGED=$?

validate_sshd_config "$SSHD_CONFIG_FILE"
SYNTAX_VALID=$?

if [ $SYNTAX_VALID -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Error: Failed to validate SSH configuration"
    exit 1
fi

restart_sshd_if_needed $CONFIG_CHANGED

echo "SSH server configuration complete. Empty password logins are now forbidden."

# This script adds persistent audit rules for kernel module loading by ordinary users

AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/module_load.rules"
AUDIT_KEY="module_load_tracking"

# Check if running as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure audit rules directory exists
mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"

# Function to check if a rule already exists
rule_exists() {
    local rule_pattern="$1"
    if grep -q "${rule_pattern}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Create or update the audit rules file
# We'll remove old rules if they exist and add new ones

# Check system architecture
if [[ $(uname -m) == "x86_64" ]]; then
    # 64-bit system - add both 32-bit and 64-bit rules
    SYSCALLS_64="init_module,finit_module"
    SYSCALLS_32="init_module,finit_module"
    ARCH_64="b64"
    ARCH_32="b32"
    
    # Create/update the rules file with header if it doesn't exist
    if [[ ! -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]]; then
        cat > "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" << 'EOF'
# Audit rules for kernel module loading by ordinary users
# This rule tracks both 32-bit and 64-bit syscalls for module loading

EOF
    fi
    
    # Remove any existing rules with our key to make it idempotent
    if grep -q "key=${AUDIT_KEY}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i "/key=${AUDIT_KEY}/d" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    fi
    
    # Add 64-bit rule for ordinary users (UID >= 1000)
    echo "-a always,exit -F arch=${ARCH_64} -S ${SYSCALLS_64} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k ${AUDIT_KEY}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    
    # Add 32-bit rule for ordinary users (UID >= 1000)
    echo "-a always,exit -F arch=${ARCH_32} -S ${SYSCALLS_32} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k ${AUDIT_KEY}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    
elif [[ $(uname -m) == "i?86" ]] || [[ $(uname -m) == "i386" ]]; then
    # 32-bit system
    SYSCALLS="init_module,finit_module"
    ARCH="b32"
    
    # Create/update the rules file with header if it doesn't exist
    if [[ ! -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]]; then
        cat > "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" << 'EOF'
# Audit rules for kernel module loading by ordinary users
# This rule tracks 32-bit syscalls for module loading

EOF
    fi
    
    # Remove any existing rules with our key to make it idempotent
    if grep -q "key=${AUDIT_KEY}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i "/key=${AUDIT_KEY}/d" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    fi
    
    # Add 32-bit rule for ordinary users (UID >= 1000)
    echo "-a always,exit -F arch=${ARCH} -S ${SYSCALLS} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k ${AUDIT_KEY}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    
elif [[ $(uname -m) == "aarch64" ]]; then
    # ARM 64-bit system
    SYSCALLS="finit_module,init_module"
    ARCH="b64"
    
    # Create/update the rules file with header if it doesn't exist
    if [[ ! -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]]; then
        cat > "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" << 'EOF'
# Audit rules for kernel module loading by ordinary users
# This rule tracks 64-bit ARM syscalls for module loading

EOF
    fi
    
    # Remove any existing rules with our key to make it idempotent
    if grep -q "key=${AUDIT_KEY}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i "/key=${AUDIT_KEY}/d" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    fi
    
    # Add 64-bit rule for ordinary users (UID >= 1000)
    echo "-a always,exit -F arch=${ARCH} -S ${SYSCALLS} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k ${AUDIT_KEY}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Ensure proper permissions on the rules file
chmod 640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
chown root:root "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Load the new rules into the running audit daemon
# This applies the rules immediately without requiring a reboot
if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
    # RHEL 8+ uses augenrules
    augenrules --load
    augenrules --check
elif command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
    # Fallback to auditctl for older systems
    auditctl -R "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Ensure auditd service is enabled and running
systemctl enable auditd
systemctl restart auditd

echo "Audit rules for kernel module loading by ordinary users have been successfully configured."
exit 0

    echo "-a always,exit -F arch=${ARCH} -S ${SYSCALLS} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k ${AUDIT_KEY}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Ensure proper permissions on the rules file
chmod 640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
chown root:root "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Load the new rules into the running audit daemon
# This applies the rules immediately without requiring a reboot
if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
    # RHEL 8+ uses augenrules
    augenrules --load
    augenrules --check
elif command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
    # Fallback to auditctl for older systems
    auditctl -R "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Ensure auditd service is enabled and running
systemctl enable auditd
systemctl restart auditd

echo "Audit rules for kernel module loading by ordinary users have been successfully configured."
exit 0

-a exit,always -F arch=b32 -S open -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -F exit=-EPERM -F key=access

#!/bin/bash

# Remediation script to configure audit rules for fchown system calls
# This script ensures audit records are created for successful fchown calls

# Define the audit rule for fchown
# The rule captures successful calls to fchown and fchownat on both 32-bit and 64-bit interfaces
# for users with login UIDs >= 1000, tagged with the 'perm_mod' identifier

AUDIT_RULE_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules"
AUDIT_SYSTEM_RULES="/etc/audit/rules.d/99-finalize.rules"

# Ensure audit directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Define the audit rules for fchown syscalls
# fchown (32-bit: 95, 64-bit: 93) - changes file ownership via open file descriptor
FCHOWN_RULE="
-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -F key=perm_mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -F key=perm_mod
"

# Function to add rules to audit.rules if not already present
add_audit_rules() {
    local rule_file="$1"
    local rule_content="$2"
    
    # Create the file if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "$rule_file" ]; then
        touch "$rule_file"
    fi
    
    # Add each rule line if it doesn't already exist
    while IFS= read -r rule_line; do
        # Skip empty lines
        if [ -z "$rule_line" ]; then
            continue
        fi
        
        # Check if the rule already exists in the file
        if ! grep -q "^$(echo "$rule_line" | sed 's/[]\/$*.^[]/\\&/g')$" "$rule_file" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "$rule_line" >> "$rule_file"
        fi
    done <<< "$rule_content"
}

# Function to load audit rules
load_audit_rules() {
    # Load the audit rules using augenrules
    if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
        augenrules --load
    # Fallback to auditctl if augenrules is not available
    elif command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
        auditctl -R "$AUDIT_RULE_FILE"
    fi
}

# Main script execution
echo "Configuring audit rules for fchown system calls..."

# Add the audit rules to the rules file
add_audit_rules "$AUDIT_RULE_FILE" "$FCHOWN_RULE"

# Ensure the audit daemon is enabled and running
systemctl enable auditd
systemctl restart auditd

# Load the audit rules
if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
    augenrules --load
    auditctl -l | grep -q "fchown" && echo "Audit rules loaded successfully" || echo "Warning: Failed to load some audit rules"
elif command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
    auditctl -R "$AUDIT_RULE_FILE"
    auditctl -l | grep -q "fchown" && echo "Audit rules loaded successfully" || echo "Warning: Failed to load some audit rules"
else
    echo "Error: Neither augenrules nor auditctl found"
    exit 1
fi

# Verify the rules are in place
echo "Verifying audit rules configuration..."
echo "Current audit rules for fchown:"
auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -E "fchown|perm_mod" || true

echo "Audit configuration complete and persisted."
