#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Script to generate and apply USBGuard policy based on currently connected USB devices
# This is idempotent and ensures the policy persists across reboots

# Check if running as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root"
    exit 1
fi

# Install USBGuard if not already installed
if ! command -v usbguard &> /dev/null; then
    echo "Installing USBGuard..."
    yum install -y usbguard
fi

# Define the policy file path
POLICY_FILE="/etc/usbguard/rules.conf"
POLICY_BACKUP="${POLICY_FILE}.backup.$(date +%s)"

# Create a temporary policy file with all currently connected USB devices allowed
TEMP_POLICY=$(mktemp)
trap "rm -f $TEMP_POLICY" EXIT

# Generate rules for all currently connected USB devices
echo "# USBGuard policy generated from currently connected devices" > "$TEMP_POLICY"
echo "# Generated on $(date)" >> "$TEMP_POLICY"
echo "" >> "$TEMP_POLICY"

# Add a catch-all rule at the end to reject unknown devices
echo "# Reject all other devices" >> "$TEMP_POLICY"
echo "reject" >> "$TEMP_POLICY"

# Use usbguard generate-policy to create rules for currently connected devices
if command -v usbguard &> /dev/null; then
    # Generate policy rules for connected devices
    GENERATED_RULES=$(usbguard generate-policy 2>/dev/null || true)
    
    if [ -n "$GENERATED_RULES" ]; then
        # Insert the generated rules before the final reject rule
        echo "# Allow currently connected USB devices" > "$TEMP_POLICY"
        echo "# Generated on $(date)" >> "$TEMP_POLICY"
        echo "" >> "$TEMP_POLICY"
        echo "$GENERATED_RULES" >> "$TEMP_POLICY"
        echo "" >> "$TEMP_POLICY"
        echo "# Reject all other devices" >> "$TEMP_POLICY"
        echo "reject" >> "$TEMP_POLICY"
    fi
fi

# Check if policy file exists and is different from what we want to apply
if [ -f "$POLICY_FILE" ]; then
    # Compare policies, ignoring comments and timestamps
    CURRENT_RULES=$(grep -v "^#" "$POLICY_FILE" | grep -v "^$" || true)
    NEW_RULES=$(grep -v "^#" "$TEMP_POLICY" | grep -v "^$" || true)
    
    if [ "$CURRENT_RULES" != "$NEW_RULES" ]; then
        echo "Updating USBGuard policy..."
        # Backup existing policy
        cp "$POLICY_FILE" "$POLICY_BACKUP"
        echo "Backed up existing policy to $POLICY_BACKUP"
    else
        echo "USBGuard policy is already current"
        exit 0
    fi
else
    echo "Creating new USBGuard policy..."
fi

# Apply the new policy
cp "$TEMP_POLICY" "$POLICY_FILE"
chmod 600 "$POLICY_FILE"
chown root:root "$POLICY_FILE"

# Ensure USBGuard service is enabled and running
systemctl enable usbguard || true
systemctl restart usbguard || true

echo "USBGuard policy has been successfully applied and will persist across reboots"

#!/bin/bash

# This script disables IPv6 router advertisements on all interfaces
# It applies the change at runtime and ensures persistence across reboots

set -e

# Function to set sysctl parameter
set_sysctl_param() {
    local param="$1"
    local value="$2"
    
    # Check if parameter already has the correct value
    current_value=$(sysctl -n "$param" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    
    if [ "$current_value" != "$value" ]; then
        # Set the value at runtime
        sysctl -w "$param=$value" > /dev/null 2>&1
        
        # Ensure it persists across reboots by modifying sysctl config
        sysctl_file="/etc/sysctl.d/99-ipv6-ra-disable.conf"
        
        # Create or update the sysctl configuration file
        if ! grep -q "^$param" "$sysctl_file" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "$param = $value" >> "$sysctl_file"
        else
            sed -i "s/^$param.*/$param = $value/" "$sysctl_file"
        fi
    fi
}

# Ensure the sysctl configuration file exists
sysctl_file="/etc/sysctl.d/99-ipv6-ra-disable.conf"
if [ ! -f "$sysctl_file" ]; then
    touch "$sysctl_file"
    chmod 644 "$sysctl_file"
fi

# Disable IPv6 router advertisements globally
set_sysctl_param "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra" "0"

# Disable IPv6 router advertisements on the default interface
set_sysctl_param "net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra" "0"

# Get list of all IPv6-capable interfaces and disable RA on each
for interface in $(ip -6 link show | grep "^[0-9]" | awk '{print $2}' | sed 's/:$//'); do
    set_sysctl_param "net.ipv6.conf.$interface.accept_ra" "0"
done

# Apply sysctl settings from the configuration file
sysctl -p "$sysctl_file" > /dev/null 2>&1

exit 0

# Check if running as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root"
    exit 1
fi

# Apply noexec mount option to /dev/shm immediately
echo "Applying noexec option to /dev/shm..."
mount -o remount,noexec /dev/shm 2>/dev/null || true

# Add or update fstab entry to persist the noexec option
FSTAB="/etc/fstab"
SHMMOUNT="tmpfs.*\/dev\/shm"
NOEXEC_OPTION="noexec"

# Check if /dev/shm entry exists in fstab
if grep -q "$SHMMOUNT" "$FSTAB"; then
    # Entry exists, check if noexec is already set
    if grep "$SHMMOUNT" "$FSTAB" | grep -q "$NOEXEC_OPTION"; then
        echo "/dev/shm already has noexec option in $FSTAB"
    else
        # Add noexec to existing options
        sed -i "/$SHMMOUNT/s/defaults/defaults,noexec/" "$FSTAB"
        echo "Added noexec option to existing /dev/shm entry in $FSTAB"
    fi
else
    # Entry doesn't exist, add it
    echo "tmpfs                   /dev/shm                tmpfs   defaults,noexec 0 0" >> "$FSTAB"
    echo "Added /dev/shm entry with noexec option to $FSTAB"
fi

# Verify the configuration
echo ""
echo "Verification:"
echo "============"
echo "Current mount options for /dev/shm:"
mount | grep "/dev/shm" || echo "Warning: /dev/shm not found in mount output"

echo ""
echo "Entry in $FSTAB:"
grep "$SHMMOUNT" "$FSTAB" || echo "Warning: /dev/shm not found in $FSTAB"

# Test that execution is blocked
echo ""
echo "Testing that execution is blocked on /dev/shm..."
TEST_FILE="/dev/shm/test_exec_$RANDOM"
echo "#!/bin/bash" > "$TEST_FILE"
echo "echo 'This should not execute'" >> "$TEST_FILE"
chmod +x "$TEST_FILE"

if ! "$TEST_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "✓ Successfully prevented execution on /dev/shm"
    rm -f "$TEST_FILE"
else
    echo "✗ Warning: Execution was not blocked on /dev/shm"
    rm -f "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "Remediation completed successfully"

    echo "This script must be run as root"
    exit 1
fi

# Create the directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Define the audit rules to add
# These rules capture failed attempts (EPERM and EACCES) by regular users (uid >= 1000)
# for open_by_handle_at syscall on both 32-bit and 64-bit architectures
RULE_CONTENT="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open_by_handle_at -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -F exit=-EACCES -F key=access
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open_by_handle_at -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -F exit=-EPERM -F key=access
-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open_by_handle_at -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -F exit=-EACCES -F key=access
-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open_by_handle_at -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -F exit=-EPERM -F key=access"

# Check if the rules file already exists and contains the required rules
if [[ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]]; then
    # Check if all required rules are already present
    rules_present=true
    while IFS= read -r rule; do
        if ! grep -qF "$rule" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"; then
            rules_present=false
            break
        fi
    done <<< "$RULE_CONTENT"
    
    if [[ "$rules_present" == true ]]; then
        echo "Audit rules are already properly configured in $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
        exit 0
    fi
fi

# Write the rules to the audit rules file

# Set appropriate permissions on the rules file
chmod 640 "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# Load the new rules
augenrules --load

# Restart the audit daemon to ensure rules are applied
systemctl restart auditd

# Remediation script to prevent authentication with empty passwords on RHEL 8

set -e

# Check if running as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Function to check if line exists in file
line_exists_in_file() {
    local line="$1"
    local file="$2"
    grep -qF "$line" "$file" 2>/dev/null
}

# Function to add or update line in file
ensure_line_in_file() {
    local line="$1"
    local file="$2"
    
    if ! line_exists_in_file "$line" "$file"; then
        echo "$line" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Configure PAM to deny empty passwords
PAM_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/pam.d"

# Handle system-auth for password authentication
if [[ -f "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/system-auth" ]]; then
    # Backup the file
    cp "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/system-auth" "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/system-auth.backup.$(date +%s)"
    
    # Check if pam_unix.so with nullok is present
    if grep -q "pam_unix.so.*nullok" "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/system-auth"; then
        # Remove nullok parameter from pam_unix.so
        sed -i 's/\(pam_unix\.so[^n]*\)nullok[^[:space:]]*/\1/g' "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/system-auth"
        sed -i 's/[[:space:]]*$//' "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/system-auth"
    fi
fi

# Handle password-auth for password authentication
if [[ -f "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/password-auth" ]]; then
    # Backup the file
    cp "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/password-auth" "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/password-auth.backup.$(date +%s)"
    
    # Check if pam_unix.so with nullok is present
    if grep -q "pam_unix.so.*nullok" "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/password-auth"; then
        # Remove nullok parameter from pam_unix.so
        sed -i 's/\(pam_unix\.so[^n]*\)nullok[^[:space:]]*/\1/g' "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/password-auth"
        sed -i 's/[[:space:]]*$//' "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/password-auth"
    fi

# Handle SSH configuration to prevent empty password logins
SSH_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
if [[ -f "$SSH_CONFIG" ]]; then
    # Backup the file
    cp "$SSH_CONFIG" "$SSH_CONFIG.backup.$(date +%s)"

    if grep -q "^PermitEmptyPasswords" "$SSH_CONFIG"; then

    else
        echo "PermitEmptyPasswords no" >> "$SSH_CONFIG"
    fi

    if sshd -t 2>/dev/null; then
        # Restart SSH service if configuration is valid
        systemctl restart sshd || true
    else
        echo "Warning: SSH configuration validation failed" >&2
        # Restore backup
        cp "$SSH_CONFIG.backup.$(date +%s | cut -c1-10)000000" "$SSH_CONFIG" || true
    fi
fi

# Handle login.defs to set password policy
LOGIN_DEFS="/etc/login.defs"
if [[ -f "$LOGIN_DEFS" ]]; then
    # Backup the file
    cp "$LOGIN_DEFS" "$LOGIN_DEFS.backup.$(date +%s)"
    
    # Ensure PASS_MIN_LEN is set appropriately
    if grep -q "^PASS_MIN_LEN" "$LOGIN_DEFS"; then
        sed -i 's/^PASS_MIN_LEN.*/PASS_MIN_LEN 1/' "$LOGIN_DEFS"
    else
        echo "PASS_MIN_LEN 1" >> "$LOGIN_DEFS"
    fi
fi

# Handle PAM pam_cracklib for password strength (if available)
if [[ -f "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/system-password" ]]; then
    # Backup the file
    cp "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/system-password" "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/system-password.backup.$(date +%s)"
    
    # Add pam_cracklib or pam_pwquality to deny weak passwords
    if ! grep -q "pam_pwquality.so" "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/system-password"; then
        if ! grep -q "pam_cracklib.so" "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/system-password"; then
            # Try to add pam_pwquality (newer systems)
            sed -i '1ipasword requisite pam_pwquality.so try_first_pass local_users_only dcredit=-1 ucredit=-1 ocredit=-1 lcredit=-1' "$PAM_CONFIG_DIR/system-password" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    fi
fi

# Create or update /etc/security/pwquality.conf if it exists
PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
if [[ -f "$PWQUALITY_CONF" ]]; then
    # Backup the file
    cp "$PWQUALITY_CONF" "$PWQUALITY_CONF.backup.$(date +%s)"
    
    # Ensure minlen is set to at least 1
    if grep -q "^minlen" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"; then
        sed -i 's/^minlen.*/minlen = 1/' "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
    else
        echo "minlen = 1" >> "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
    fi
fi

echo "System has been configured to refuse authentication with empty passwords"
exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Set the failed login tracking interval to at least 900 seconds on RHEL 8
# This script configures pam_faillock to set the interval parameter

set -e

# Ensure we're running on RHEL 8
if ! grep -q "VERSION_ID=\"8" /etc/os-release 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "This script is designed for RHEL 8 systems"
    exit 1
fi

# Install necessary packages if not already installed
if ! rpm -q pam 2>/dev/null | grep -q pam; then
    dnf install -y pam
fi

# Ensure pam_faillock module is available
if ! rpm -q pam 2>/dev/null | grep -q pam-; then
    dnf install -y "pam-*"
fi

# Define the PAM configuration file for system-auth
PAM_FILE="/etc/pam.d/system-auth"
PAM_AUTH_AC_FILE="/etc/pam.d/system-auth-ac"

# Function to update or add faillock configuration in a PAM file
update_pam_faillock() {
    local pam_file=$1
    local auth_line_pattern="auth.*pam_faillock.so.*preauth"
    local deny_pattern="deny=5"
    local interval_pattern="interval=900"
    
    if [ ! -f "$pam_file" ]; then
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Create backup if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "${pam_file}.bak" ]; then
        cp "$pam_file" "${pam_file}.bak"
    fi
    
    # Check if pam_faillock preauth line exists
    if grep -q "pam_faillock.so.*preauth" "$pam_file"; then
        # Check if interval is already set
        if grep -q "pam_faillock.so.*preauth.*interval=900" "$pam_file"; then
            return 0
        fi
        
        # Add or update interval parameter
        sed -i "/pam_faillock.so.*preauth/ {
            /interval=/! s/\(pam_faillock.so[^#]*\)$/\1 interval=900/
            s/interval=[0-9]*/interval=900/
        }" "$pam_file"
    else
        # Add the preauth line if it doesn't exist
        sed -i "/^auth.*required.*pam_unix.so/ i auth        required                                     pam_faillock.so preauth silent audit deny=5 unlock_time=900 interval=900" "$pam_file"
    fi
}

# Check if we're using system-auth or system-auth-ac
if [ -f "$PAM_AUTH_AC_FILE" ]; then
    update_pam_faillock "$PAM_AUTH_AC_FILE"
else
    update_pam_faillock "$PAM_FILE"
fi

# Also ensure the faillock configuration file exists and has proper settings
FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"

# Create or update /etc/security/faillock.conf
if [ ! -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ]; then
    cat > "$FAILLOCK_CONF" << 'EOF'
# /etc/security/faillock.conf
# Configuration file for pam_faillock module
deny = 5
unlock_time = 900
interval = 900
EOF
    chmod 644 "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
else
    # Update existing file to ensure interval is set
    if grep -q "^interval" "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        sed -i 's/^interval[[:space:]]*=.*/interval = 900/' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    else
        echo "interval = 900" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
fi

# Ensure faillock directory exists with proper permissions
if [ ! -d "/var/run/faillock" ]; then
    mkdir -p /var/run/faillock
    chmod 700 /var/run/faillock
fi

# Create a systemd unit to ensure faillock directory persists across reboots
cat > /etc/tmpfiles.d/faillock.conf << 'EOF'
d /var/run/faillock 0700 root root
EOF

# Apply the tmpfiles configuration
systemd-tmpfiles --create /etc/tmpfiles.d/faillock.conf 2>/dev/null || true

# Verify the configuration was applied correctly
if grep -q "interval.*900" "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
    echo "Faillock interval successfully configured to 900 seconds"
else
    echo "Warning: Unable to verify faillock interval configuration"
    exit 1
fi

# Display the current configuration for verification
echo "Current faillock configuration:"
cat "$FAILLOCK_CONF"

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# RHEL 8 GNOME Screensaver Inactivity Timeout Configuration Script
# This script configures and locks the screensaver to activate after 15 minutes of inactivity

set -e

# Function to check if running as root
check_root() {
    if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
        echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
}

# Function to install required packages
install_packages() {
    local packages=("dconf" "gdm" "gnome-shell")
    echo "Checking and installing required packages..."
    
    for pkg in "${packages[@]}"; do
        if ! rpm -q "$pkg" &> /dev/null; then
            echo "Installing $pkg..."
            dnf install -y "$pkg" > /dev/null 2>&1
        fi
    done
}

# Function to configure dconf settings for GNOME screensaver
configure_screensaver_timeout() {
    echo "Configuring screensaver inactivity timeout..."
    
    # Create dconf profile directory if it doesn't exist
    local profile_dir="/etc/dconf/profile"
    local db_dir="/etc/dconf/db"
    
    mkdir -p "$profile_dir" "$db_dir"
    
    # Create or update the user profile
    cat > "$profile_dir/user" << 'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF

    # Create the local database directory
    mkdir -p "$db_dir/local.d"
    
    # Create the screensaver configuration file
    cat > "$db_dir/local.d/screensaver" << 'EOF'
[org/gnome/desktop/session]
idle-delay=uint32 900

[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-enabled=true
lock-delay=uint32 0
active=true
EOF

    # Create locks for the settings
    mkdir -p "$db_dir/local.d/locks"
    cat > "$db_dir/local.d/locks/screensaver" << 'EOF'
/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-enabled
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/active
EOF

    # Update dconf database
    echo "Updating dconf database..."
    dconf update
}

# Function to configure GDM settings
configure_gdm_settings() {
    echo "Configuring GDM settings..."
    
    # Ensure GDM configuration directory exists
    mkdir -p /etc/gdm/
    
    # Update GDM configuration for idle timeout
    if [[ -f /etc/gdm/custom.conf ]]; then
        # Backup original file
        cp /etc/gdm/custom.conf /etc/gdm/custom.conf.bak
        
        # Add idle timeout setting if not present
        if ! grep -q "^IdleDelay=" /etc/gdm/custom.conf; then
            sed -i '/^\[daemon\]/a IdleDelay=900' /etc/gdm/custom.conf
        else
            sed -i 's/^IdleDelay=.*/IdleDelay=900/' /etc/gdm/custom.conf
        fi
    fi
}

# Function to configure GNOME settings via gsettings for system-wide defaults
configure_gsettings_defaults() {
    echo "Configuring GNOME settings defaults..."
    
    # Create directory for GNOME settings defaults
    mkdir -p /usr/share/glib-2.0/schemas/
    
    # Create a schema override file for system defaults
    cat > /etc/dconf/db/gdm.d/01-screensaver << 'EOF'
[org/gnome/desktop/session]
idle-delay=uint32 900

[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-enabled=true
lock-delay=uint32 0
active=true
EOF
}

# Function to ensure dconf is properly updated
finalize_dconf() {
    echo "Finalizing dconf configuration..."
    
    # Update all dconf databases
    dconf update 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Function to verify configuration
verify_configuration() {
    echo "Verifying configuration..."
    
    # Check if dconf files exist
    if [[ -f /etc/dconf/db/local.d/screensaver ]]; then
        echo "✓ Screensaver configuration file created"
    else
        echo "✗ Screensaver configuration file not found"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if locks file exists
    if [[ -f /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks/screensaver ]]; then
        echo "✓ Screensaver locks configuration created"
    else
        echo "✗ Screensaver locks not found"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if dconf profile exists
    if [[ -f /etc/dconf/profile/user ]]; then
        echo "✓ dconf user profile created"
    else
        echo "✗ dconf user profile not found"
        return 1
    fi
    
    return 0
}

# Main execution
main() {
    echo "Starting RHEL 8 GNOME screensaver configuration..."
    
    check_root
    install_packages
    configure_screensaver_timeout
    configure_gdm_settings
    configure_gsettings_defaults
    finalize_dconf
    verify_configuration
    
    echo ""
    echo "Configuration completed successfully!"
    echo "The screensaver will now activate after 15 minutes of inactivity."
    echo "This setting is locked and cannot be overridden by individual users."
    echo ""
    echo "Note: Changes will take effect after users log out and log back in."
}

# Run main function
main
exit 0

    finalize_dconf
    verify_configuration
    
    echo ""
    echo "Configuration completed successfully!"
    echo "The screensaver will now activate after 15 minutes of inactivity."
    echo "This setting is locked and cannot be overridden by individual users."
    echo ""
    echo "Note: Changes will take effect after users log out and log back in."

main
exit 0

# Remediation script to ensure /dev/shm filesystem disallows device-file interpretation
# This script is idempotent and applies changes immediately and persistently

set -e

# Check if /dev/shm is mounted
if ! mountpoint -q /dev/shm; then
    echo "Error: /dev/shm is not mounted"
    exit 1
fi

# Get current mount options for /dev/shm
CURRENT_OPTIONS=$(mount | grep '/dev/shm' | grep -oP '(?<=\().*(?=\))')

if echo "$CURRENT_OPTIONS" | grep -q 'nodev'; then
    echo "/dev/shm already has nodev option enabled"
    # Still ensure persistence in fstab
else
    # Remount /dev/shm with nodev option
    mount -o remount,nodev /dev/shm
    echo "Successfully remounted /dev/shm with nodev option"
fi

# First, check if /dev/shm entry exists in /etc/fstab
if grep -q '^[^#]*[[:space:]]/dev/shm[[:space:]]' /etc/fstab; then
    # Entry exists, update it to include nodev if not already present
    if grep '^[^#]*[[:space:]]/dev/shm[[:space:]]' /etc/fstab | grep -q 'nodev'; then

        sed -i '/^[^#]*[[:space:]]\/dev\/shm[[:space:]]/s/\([[:space:]].*[[:space:]]\)\([^,]*\)$/\1\2,nodev/' /etc/fstab
        # More robust approach: extract the line, modify it, and replace
        FSTAB_LINE=$(grep '^[^#]*[[:space:]]/dev/shm[[:space:]]' /etc/fstab)
        if ! echo "$FSTAB_LINE" | grep -q 'nodev'; then

            sed -i '/^[^#]*[[:space:]]\/dev\/shm[[:space:]]/d' /etc/fstab

            OPTIONS=$(echo "$FSTAB_LINE" | awk '{print $4}')

            else

            echo "$DEVICE $MOUNT_POINT $FSTYPE $NEW_OPTIONS $DUMP $PASS" >> /etc/fstab

mount | grep '/dev/shm'

echo "Verifying /etc/fstab entry for /dev/shm:"
grep '^[^#]*[[:space:]]/dev/shm[[:space:]]' /etc/fstab || true

# Remediation script for RHEL 8 IPv6 router advertisement vulnerability
# Ensures newly initialized IPv6 interfaces do not accept router advertisements

set -euo pipefail

# Function to check if a parameter needs modification
check_and_set_sysctl() {
    local param="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local current_value
    
    current_value=$(sysctl -n "$param" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    
    if [ "$current_value" != "$value" ]; then
        echo "Updating $param from $current_value to $value"
        sysctl -w "$param=$value" > /dev/null
        return 0
    else
        echo "$param is already set to $value"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to ensure sysctl configuration is persistent
ensure_persistent_sysctl() {
    local param="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local sysctl_file="/etc/sysctl.d/99-ipv6-ra-disable.conf"
    local param_escaped="${param//\./\.}"
    
    # Create the sysctl configuration file if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "$sysctl_file" ]; then
        touch "$sysctl_file"
        chmod 644 "$sysctl_file"
    fi
    
    # Check if the parameter is already in the file
    if grep -q "^${param_escaped}=" "$sysctl_file"; then
        # Update existing parameter
        sed -i "s/^${param_escaped}=.*/$param=$value/" "$sysctl_file"
    else
        # Add new parameter
        echo "$param=$value" >> "$sysctl_file"
    fi
}

echo "=== IPv6 Router Advertisement Remediation for RHEL 8 ==="
echo ""

# Disable IPv6 router advertisement acceptance on all interfaces
echo "Step 1: Disabling IPv6 router advertisements..."

# Set for all interfaces (both existing and new)
check_and_set_sysctl "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra" "0"
ensure_persistent_sysctl "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra" "0"

# Also set for the default interface configuration (applies to newly created interfaces)
check_and_set_sysctl "net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra" "0"
ensure_persistent_sysctl "net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra" "0"

echo ""
echo "Step 2: Verifying runtime configuration..."

# Verify the settings were applied
all_ra=$(sysctl -n net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra)
default_ra=$(sysctl -n net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra)

if [ "$all_ra" = "0" ] && [ "$default_ra" = "0" ]; then
    echo "✓ Runtime configuration verified successfully"
else
    echo "✗ Failed to apply runtime configuration"
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "Step 3: Verifying persistent configuration..."

if grep -q "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra=0" /etc/sysctl.d/99-ipv6-ra-disable.conf && \
   grep -q "net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra=0" /etc/sysctl.d/99-ipv6-ra-disable.conf; then
    echo "✓ Persistent configuration verified successfully"
else
    echo "✗ Failed to apply persistent configuration"
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "Step 4: Applying configuration to existing interfaces..."

# For each existing interface, ensure it has accept_ra disabled
for interface in $(ip -o link show | awk -F': ' '{print $2}' | grep -v "^lo$"); do
    param="net.ipv6.conf.${interface}.accept_ra"
    check_and_set_sysctl "$param" "0"
done

echo ""
echo "=== Remediation Complete ==="
echo "IPv6 router advertisements are now disabled on all interfaces"
echo "This setting will persist across system reboots"

exit 0

set -e

# Script to configure GnuTLS to honor system-wide cryptographic policy
# This ensures GnuTLS does not permit weak/legacy protocol versions

# Define the GnuTLS configuration file
GNUTLS_CONFIG_FILE="/etc/gnutls/default-priorities.txt"
GNUTLS_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/gnutls"

# Create the configuration directory if it doesn't exist
if [[ ! -d "$GNUTLS_CONFIG_DIR" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$GNUTLS_CONFIG_DIR"
fi

# Define the secure GnuTLS priority string that defers to system policy
# This string explicitly disables weak protocols and uses secure defaults
SECURE_PRIORITY_STRING="SYSTEM"

# Check if the config file exists
if [[ ! -f "$GNUTLS_CONFIG_FILE" ]]; then
    # Create the file with the secure configuration
    echo "Creating GnuTLS configuration file: $GNUTLS_CONFIG_FILE"
    echo "$SECURE_PRIORITY_STRING" > "$GNUTLS_CONFIG_FILE"
else
    # Check if the file contains the correct configuration
    CURRENT_CONTENT=$(cat "$GNUTLS_CONFIG_FILE" | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*$' | grep -v '^#')
    
    if [[ "$CURRENT_CONTENT" != "$SECURE_PRIORITY_STRING" ]]; then
        echo "Updating GnuTLS configuration to use system-wide crypto policy"
        # Backup the existing configuration
        cp "$GNUTLS_CONFIG_FILE" "${GNUTLS_CONFIG_FILE}.backup.$(date +%s)"
        # Write the secure configuration
        echo "$SECURE_PRIORITY_STRING" > "$GNUTLS_CONFIG_FILE"
    else
        echo "GnuTLS configuration is already compliant"
    fi
fi

# Ensure proper permissions on the configuration file
chmod 644 "$GNUTLS_CONFIG_FILE"

# Verify the system-wide crypto policy is in place
POLICY_FILE="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/gnutls.config"

if [[ -f "$POLICY_FILE" ]]; then
    echo "System crypto policy for GnuTLS found at: $POLICY_FILE"
    # Display current policy
    echo "Current policy settings:"
    cat "$POLICY_FILE" || echo "Unable to read policy file"
else
    echo "Applying system-wide crypto policy"
    # Run update-crypto-policies to generate the backend-specific policy
    if command -v update-crypto-policies &> /dev/null; then
        update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT || true
    else
        echo "Warning: update-crypto-policies command not found"
    fi
fi

# Check if gnutls-cli is available and test the configuration
if command -v gnutls-cli &> /dev/null; then
    echo "Testing GnuTLS priority settings..."
    # This will show the priority string being used
    PRIORITY_TEST=$(gnutls-cli --priority-list 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "Unable to test priorities")
    echo "GnuTLS is configured with priorities"
else
    echo "gnutls-cli not found, skipping priority test"
fi

# Ensure GnuTLS library cache is cleared to apply changes
if [[ -d "/var/cache/gnutls" ]]; then
    echo "Clearing GnuTLS cache"
    rm -rf /var/cache/gnutls/* 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Verify that the environment variable for GnuTLS is not overriding the policy
# Check common shell profile files
for profile_file in /etc/profile /etc/profile.d/*.sh /etc/bashrc /etc/bash.bashrc; do
    if [[ -f "$profile_file" ]]; then
        if grep -q "GNUTLS_SYSTEM_PRIORITY_FILE" "$profile_file" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "Found GNUTLS_SYSTEM_PRIORITY_FILE in $profile_file"
            # Ensure it's not set to override with weak policies
            if ! grep -q "GNUTLS_SYSTEM_PRIORITY_FILE=/etc/gnutls/default-priorities.txt" "$profile_file" 2>/dev/null; then
                sed -i '/GNUTLS_SYSTEM_PRIORITY_FILE=/d' "$profile_file"
            fi
        fi
    fi
done

# Create a systemd drop-in directory for gnutls services if they exist
GNUTLS_SERVICE_DIRS=(
    "/etc/systemd/system/cups.service.d"
    "/etc/systemd/system/dovecot.service.d"
    "/etc/systemd/system/postfix.service.d"
    "/etc/systemd/system/apache2.service.d"
    "/etc/systemd/system/nginx.service.d"
)

# Ensure that any GnuTLS-using services inherit system crypto policy
for service_dir in "${GNUTLS_SERVICE_DIRS[@]}"; do
    if [[ -d "$service_dir" ]]; then
        SERVICE_OVERRIDE="${service_dir}/gnutls-policy.conf"
        if [[ ! -f "$SERVICE_OVERRIDE" ]]; then
            mkdir -p "$service_dir"
            cat > "$SERVICE_OVERRIDE" << 'EOF'
[Service]
# Ensure GnuTLS services use system crypto policy
Environment="GNUTLS_SYSTEM_PRIORITY_FILE=/etc/gnutls/default-priorities.txt"
EOF
        fi
    fi
done

echo "GnuTLS configuration has been updated to comply with system-wide cryptographic policy"
echo "The configuration now honors the centralized system policy and rejects legacy protocols"

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to configure RHEL 8 audit rules for file ownership changes
# This script configures the audit daemon to capture chown system calls for non-system users

set -e

# Define the audit rules file path
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules"
AUDIT_RULES_BACKUP="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules.bak"

# Define the audit rule key for permission modifications
RULE_KEY="perm_mod"

# Function to check if audit service is installed
check_audit_installed() {
    if ! command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
        echo "Installing audit daemon..."
        dnf install -y audit audit-libs
    fi
}

# Function to check if a rule already exists
rule_exists() {
    local rule="$1"
    if grep -F "$rule" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to add audit rules
add_audit_rules() {
    local rule_32bit="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S chown -S fchown -S fchownat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k $RULE_KEY"
    local rule_64bit="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S chown -S fchown -S fchownat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k $RULE_KEY"
    
    # Backup the original file if it exists
    if [ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]; then
        if [ ! -f "$AUDIT_RULES_BACKUP" ]; then
            cp "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" "$AUDIT_RULES_BACKUP"
        fi
    else
        # Create the rules directory and file if they don't exist
        mkdir -p "$(dirname "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE")"
        touch "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    fi
    
    # Remove existing audit rules for chown to ensure idempotency
    if grep -q "chown" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i "/chown/d" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    fi

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to configure RHEL 8 audit rules for file ownership changes
# This script configures the audit daemon to capture chown system calls for non-system users

set -e

# Define the audit rules file path
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules"
AUDIT_RULES_BACKUP="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules.bak"

# Define the audit rule key for permission modifications
RULE_KEY="perm_mod"

# Function to check if audit service is installed
check_audit_installed() {
    if ! command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
        echo "Installing audit daemon..."
        dnf install -y audit audit-libs
    fi
}

# Function to check if a rule already exists
rule_exists() {
    local rule="$1"
    if grep -F "$rule" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to add audit rules
add_audit_rules() {
    local rule_32bit="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S chown -S fchown -S fchownat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k $RULE_KEY"
    local rule_64bit="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S chown -S fchown -S fchownat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k $RULE_KEY"
    
    # Backup the original file if it exists
    if [ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]; then
        if [ ! -f "$AUDIT_RULES_BACKUP" ]; then
            cp "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" "$AUDIT_RULES_BACKUP"
        fi
    else
        # Create the rules directory and file if they don't exist
        mkdir -p "$(dirname "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE")"
        touch "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    fi
    
    # Remove existing audit rules for chown to ensure idempotency
    if grep -q "chown" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i "/chown/d" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    fi
    
    # Add the 32-bit audit rule if not already present
    if ! rule_exists "$rule_32bit"; then
        echo "$rule_32bit" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
        echo "Added 32-bit chown audit rule"
    fi
    
    # Add the 64-bit audit rule if not already present
    if ! rule_exists "$rule_64bit"; then
        echo "$rule_64bit" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
        echo "Added 64-bit chown audit rule"
    fi
}

# Function to load audit rules
load_audit_rules() {
    # Load the rules using auditctl

    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then

}

#!/bin/bash

# Script to enforce sudo credential caching timeout on RHEL 8
# Ensures sudo requires re-authentication on a short, non-indefinite schedule

set -euo pipefail

# Define the sudo timeout value in minutes (default: 5 minutes)
SUDO_TIMEOUT="${SUDO_TIMEOUT:-5}"

# Path to sudoers configuration directory
SUDOERS_D="/etc/sudoers.d"
# Path to the main sudoers file
SUDOERS_FILE="/etc/sudoers"
# Custom sudoers drop-in file for timeout configuration
SUDO_TIMEOUT_FILE="${SUDOERS_D}/timeout"

# Function to validate sudoers syntax
validate_sudoers() {
    if ! visudo -cf "$1" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Error: Invalid sudoers syntax in $1" >&2
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

# Function to check if sudoers file already has the timeout setting
has_timeout_setting() {
    local file="$1"
    if grep -q "Defaults.*timestamp_timeout" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi
    return 1
}

# Function to remove conflicting timeout settings
remove_conflicting_settings() {
    local file="$1"
    
    # Use a temporary file for safe in-place editing
    local temp_file="${file}.tmp.$$"
    
    # Remove any lines with timestamp_timeout or timestamp_type settings
    # but preserve everything else
    grep -v "timestamp_timeout\|timestamp_type" "$file" > "$temp_file" || true
    
    # Replace original file with modified version
    mv "$temp_file" "$file"
}

# Function to add or update timeout setting
set_timeout_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local timeout="$2"
    
    # Check if file exists
    if [[ ! -f "$file" ]]; then
        # Create new file with timeout setting
        echo "Defaults timestamp_timeout=$timeout" > "$file"
        chmod 0440 "$file"
    else
        # Check if timeout setting already exists
        if has_timeout_setting "$file"; then
            # Remove conflicting settings first
            remove_conflicting_settings "$file"
            # Add new setting
            echo "Defaults timestamp_timeout=$timeout" >> "$file"
        else
            # Just append the setting
            echo "Defaults timestamp_timeout=$timeout" >> "$file"
        fi
    fi
}

# Function to ensure idempotency
ensure_idempotent_setting() {
    local timeout="$1"
    
    # First check the drop-in file
    if [[ -f "$SUDO_TIMEOUT_FILE" ]]; then
        # Remove all timestamp_timeout lines from drop-in file
        sed -i '/Defaults.*timestamp_timeout/d' "$SUDO_TIMEOUT_FILE"
    fi
    
    # Check main sudoers file for conflicting settings
    if grep -q "Defaults.*timestamp_timeout" "$SUDOERS_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        # Remove timestamp_timeout from main sudoers
        sed -i '/Defaults.*timestamp_timeout/d' "$SUDOERS_FILE"
    fi
    
    # Ensure the drop-in directory exists
    mkdir -p "$SUDOERS_D"
    
    # Add timeout setting to drop-in file
    set_timeout_in_file "$SUDO_TIMEOUT_FILE" "$timeout"
    
    # Validate the sudoers configuration
    if ! validate_sudoers "$SUDOERS_FILE"; then
        echo "Error: Main sudoers file validation failed" >&2
        return 1
    fi
    
    if ! validate_sudoers "$SUDO_TIMEOUT_FILE"; then
        echo "Error: Sudo timeout configuration validation failed" >&2
        return 1
    fi
    
    return 0
}

# Main execution
main() {
    echo "Configuring sudo credential caching timeout to ${SUDO_TIMEOUT} minutes..."
    
    # Ensure we're running as root
    if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
        echo "Error: This script must be run as root" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Apply the timeout setting idempotently
    if ensure_idempotent_setting "$SUDO_TIMEOUT"; then
        echo "Successfully configured sudo timeout to ${SUDO_TIMEOUT} minutes"
        
        # Verify the setting
        if [[ -f "$SUDO_TIMEOUT_FILE" ]]; then
            echo "Verification: Current sudo timeout setting:"
            grep "timestamp_timeout" "$SUDO_TIMEOUT_FILE" || true
        fi
        
        exit 0
    else
        echo "Error: Failed to configure sudo timeout" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
}

# Execute main function
main

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to remove Red Hat automated bug-reporting plugin from RHEL 8
# The abrtd service and ABRT (Automatic Bug Reporting Tool) package should be removed

set -e

# Function to check if package is installed
is_package_installed() {
    rpm -q "$1" &>/dev/null
    return $?
}

# Function to check if service is active
is_service_active() {
    systemctl is-active --quiet "$1" 2>/dev/null
    return $?
}

# Function to check if service is enabled
is_service_enabled() {
    systemctl is-enabled "$1" &>/dev/null
    return $?
}

# Stop the abrtd service if it's running
if is_service_active abrtd; then
    echo "Stopping abrtd service..."
    systemctl stop abrtd
fi

# Disable the abrtd service if it's enabled
if is_service_enabled abrtd; then
    echo "Disabling abrtd service..."
    systemctl disable abrtd
fi

# Remove ABRT packages if installed
ABRT_PACKAGES=("abrt" "abrt-addon-ccpp" "abrt-addon-python" "abrt-addon-vmcore" "abrt-cli" "abrt-console-notification" "abrt-dbus" "abrt-desktop" "abrt-gui" "abrt-java-connector" "abrt-libs" "abrt-retrace-client" "python3-abrt")

for package in "${ABRT_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    if is_package_installed "$package"; then
        echo "Removing package: $package"
        yum remove -y "$package" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
done

# Remove LIBREPORT packages (dependency of ABRT that handles crash reporting)
LIBREPORT_PACKAGES=("libreport" "libreport-plugin-reportuploader" "libreport-plugin-ureport" "libreport-plugin-logger")

for package in "${LIBREPORT_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    if is_package_installed "$package"; then
        echo "Removing package: $package"
        yum remove -y "$package" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
done

# Verify removal was successful
echo "Verifying ABRT removal..."
if ! is_package_installed abrt && ! is_service_active abrtd; then
    echo "Successfully removed Red Hat automated bug-reporting plugin"
    exit 0
else
    echo "Warning: Some ABRT components may still be present"
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Script to configure SHA password hashing rounds on RHEL 8

# The configuration is done via PAM (Pluggable Authentication Modules)
# We need to ensure the rounds parameter is set to at least 5000 in:
# 1. /etc/pam.d/password-auth
# 2. /etc/pam.d/system-auth

# These files may be symlinked, so we need to work with the actual files
# On RHEL 8, these are typically symlinks to files in /etc/pam.d/

set -e

# Define the minimum rounds
MIN_ROUNDS=5000

# Function to update pam file
update_pam_file() {
    local file=$1
    local rounds=$MIN_ROUNDS
    
    # Check if file exists
    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        echo "File $file does not exist, skipping"
        return
    fi
    
    # Check if the file contains pam_unix.so
    if ! grep -q "pam_unix.so" "$file"; then
        return
    fi
    
    # Create a backup
    cp "$file" "${file}.bak"
    
    # Use sed to update the rounds parameter
    # This handles multiple scenarios:
    # 1. Line with rounds already set - update to 5000 or higher
    # 2. Line with pam_unix.so but no rounds - add rounds=5000
    
    # First, handle lines that already have rounds parameter
    sed -i "s/\(pam_unix\.so.*\)rounds=[0-9]\+\(.*\)/\1rounds=$rounds\2/g" "$file"
    
    # Second, for lines with pam_unix.so but no rounds parameter
    # We need to add rounds parameter
    # This is more complex as we need to preserve the line structure
    
    local temp_file=$(mktemp)
    local modified=false
    
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        if echo "$line" | grep -q "pam_unix.so" && ! echo "$line" | grep -q "rounds="; then
            # Add rounds parameter before the closing arguments
            if echo "$line" | grep -q "sha512"; then
                line=$(echo "$line" | sed "s/sha512/sha512 rounds=$rounds/")
            else
                # Generic case - add before end of line
                line=$(echo "$line" | sed "s/$/\ rounds=$rounds/")
            fi
            modified=true
        fi
        echo "$line" >> "$temp_file"
    done < "$file"
    
    if [ "$modified" = true ]; then
        mv "$temp_file" "$file"
    else
        rm "$temp_file"
    fi
}

# Update the password-auth file
# On RHEL 8, this might be a symlink
password_auth_file="/etc/pam.d/password-auth"
if [ -L "$password_auth_file" ]; then
    # It's a symlink, resolve it
    password_auth_file=$(readlink -f "$password_auth_file")
fi
update_pam_file "$password_auth_file"

# Update the system-auth file
# On RHEL 8, this might be a symlink
system_auth_file="/etc/pam.d/system-auth"
if [ -L "$system_auth_file" ]; then
    # It's a symlink, resolve it
    system_auth_file=$(readlink -f "$system_auth_file")
fi
update_pam_file "$system_auth_file"

# Also check for password-auth-local and system-auth-local (RHEL 8 style)
for file in /etc/pam.d/password-auth-local /etc/pam.d/system-auth-local; do
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        update_pam_file "$file"
    fi
done

# Alternative approach: use authselect if available (RHEL 8)
# authselect is the preferred method on RHEL 8
if command -v authselect &> /dev/null; then
    # Check current profile
    current_profile=$(authselect current 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "")
    
    if [ -n "$current_profile" ] && [ "$current_profile" != "No" ]; then
        # authselect is in use
        # We need to ensure the system uses SHA512 with appropriate rounds
        # This is done through the authselect feature
        
        # Enable the minimal necessary features
        authselect select "$current_profile" --force 2>/dev/null || true
        
        # Update the pam files created by authselect
        for file in /etc/pam.d/password-auth /etc/pam.d/system-auth; do
            if [ -f "$file" ]; then
                # Ensure sha512 rounds are set correctly
                sed -i "s/\(pam_unix\.so.*\)rounds=[0-9]\+/\1rounds=$MIN_ROUNDS/g" "$file"
                # Add rounds if not present
                sed -i "/pam_unix\.so/s/$//" "$file"  # Remove trailing spaces
                sed -i "/pam_unix\.so.*sha512/!b; /rounds=/b; s/ *$/\ rounds=$MIN_ROUNDS/" "$file"
            fi
        done
    fi
fi

# Verify the configuration
echo "Verifying SHA hashing rounds configuration..."

# Check password-auth
if [ -f "/etc/pam.d/password-auth" ]; then
    if grep -q "pam_unix.so.*rounds=$MIN_ROUNDS" /etc/pam.d/password-auth; then
        echo "✓ password-auth configured with minimum $MIN_ROUNDS rounds"
    else
        echo "⚠ password-auth may need review"
    fi
fi

# Check system-auth  
if [ -f "/etc/pam.d/system-auth" ]; then
    if grep -q "pam_unix.so.*rounds=$MIN_ROUNDS" /etc/pam.d/system-auth; then
        echo "✓ system-auth configured with minimum $MIN_ROUNDS rounds"
    else
        echo "⚠ system-auth may need review"
    fi
fi

echo "SHA password hashing rounds configuration completed."

    password_auth_file=$(readlink -f "$password_auth_file")
fi
update_pam_file "$password_auth_file"

# Update the system-auth file
# On RHEL 8, this might be a symlink
system_auth_file="/etc/pam.d/system-auth"
if [ -L "$system_auth_file" ]; then
    # It's a symlink, resolve it
    system_auth_file=$(readlink -f "$system_auth_file")
fi
update_pam_file "$system_auth_file"

# Also check for password-auth-local and system-auth-local (RHEL 8 style)
for file in /etc/pam.d/password-auth-local /etc/pam.d/system-auth-local; do
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        update_pam_file "$file"
    fi
done

# Alternative approach: use authselect if available (RHEL 8)
# authselect is the preferred method on RHEL 8
if command -v authselect &> /dev/null; then
    # Check current profile
    current_profile=$(authselect current 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "")
    
    if [ -n "$current_profile" ] && [ "$current_profile" != "No" ]; then
        # authselect is in use
        # We need to ensure the system uses SHA512 with appropriate rounds
        # This is done through the authselect feature
        
        # Enable the minimal necessary features
        authselect select "$current_profile" --force 2>/dev/null || true
        
        # Update the pam files created by authselect
        for file in /etc/pam.d/password-auth /etc/pam.d/system-auth; do
            if [ -f "$file" ]; then
                # Ensure sha512 rounds are set correctly
                sed -i "s/\(pam_unix\.so.*\)rounds=[0-9]\+/\1rounds=$MIN_ROUNDS/g" "$file"
                # Add rounds if not present
                sed -i "/pam_unix\.so/s/$//" "$file"  # Remove trailing spaces
                sed -i "/pam_unix\.so.*sha512/!b; /rounds=/b; s/ *$/\ rounds=$MIN_ROUNDS/" "$file"
            fi
        done
    fi
fi

# Verify the configuration
echo "Verifying SHA hashing rounds configuration..."

# Check password-auth
if [ -f "/etc/pam.d/password-auth" ]; then
    if grep -q "pam_unix.so.*rounds=$MIN_ROUNDS" /etc/pam.d/password-auth; then
        echo "✓ password-auth configured with minimum $MIN_ROUNDS rounds"
    else
        echo "⚠ password-auth may need review"
    fi
fi

# Check system-auth  
if [ -f "/etc/pam.d/system-auth" ]; then
    if grep -q "pam_unix.so.*rounds=$MIN_ROUNDS" /etc/pam.d/system-auth; then
        echo "✓ system-auth configured with minimum $MIN_ROUNDS rounds"
    else
        echo "⚠ system-auth may need review"
    fi
fi

echo "SHA password hashing rounds configuration completed."

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/xattr.rules"
AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"

mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"

cat > "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" << 'EOF'
# Audit rules for extended attribute modifications (lsetxattr)
# These rules capture all lsetxattr calls for all users to detect
# potential unauthorized access attempts or abuse

# 32-bit and 64-bit lsetxattr syscalls for all users (login UID >= 1000 and root)
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S lsetxattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k perm_mod
-a exit,always -F arch=b32 -S lsetxattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k perm_mod
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S lsetxattr -F uid=0 -k perm_mod
-a exit,always -F arch=b32 -S lsetxattr -F uid=0 -k perm_mod

# Make configuration immutable to prevent tampering
-e 2
EOF

chmod 640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
chown root:root "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Load rules using augenrules if available, otherwise use auditctl
if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
    augenrules --load
    systemctl restart auditd
elif command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
    auditctl -R "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Verify the rules are loaded
if command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
    auditctl -l | grep -q "lsetxattr" || {
        echo "Error: Failed to load audit rules for lsetxattr" >&2
        exit 1
    }
fi

exit 0
